VX3iFFXBWdA.ipa: you* kən hæv ən aɪˈdiə, wɪʧ wi ˈɔfən seɪ wɪn jʊr ˈsədənli inspired.

VX3iFFXBWdA.text: I have an idea. Let's call and ask. If an idea pops into your head, you suddenly have an idea.
VX3iFFXBWdA.ipa: i* hæv ən aɪˈdiə. lɛts kɔl ənd æsk. ɪf ən aɪˈdiə pɑps ˈɪntu jʊr hɛd, ju ˈsədənli hæv ən idea.

VX3iFFXBWdA.text: Oo, an idea just popped into my head. We could go to that Chinese restaurant before the show.
VX3iFFXBWdA.ipa: oo*, ən aɪˈdiə ʤɪst pɑpt ˈɪntu maɪ hɛd. wi kʊd goʊ tɪ ðət ʧaɪˈniz ˈrɛˌstrɑnt ˌbiˈfɔr ðə show.

VX3iFFXBWdA.text: If you dream up an idea, you have a silly or unusual idea. Trust you to dream up an idea like that.
VX3iFFXBWdA.ipa: if* ju drim əp ən aɪˈdiə, ju hæv ə ˈsɪli ər ənˈjuˌʒuəl aɪˈdiə. trəst ju tɪ drim əp ən aɪˈdiə laɪk that.

VX3iFFXBWdA.text: We're vegetarian — we don't each chicken! You can put forward an idea, which means suggest an idea.
VX3iFFXBWdA.ipa: we're* ˌvɛʤəˈtɛˌriən —* wi doʊnt iʧ ˈʧɪkən! ju kən pʊt ˈfɔrwərd ən aɪˈdiə, wɪʧ minz səˈʤɛst ən idea.

VX3iFFXBWdA.text: This is often used in a more formal context, like work. I'd like to put forward an idea if I may.
VX3iFFXBWdA.ipa: this* ɪz ˈɔfən juzd ɪn ə mɔr ˈfɔrməl ˈkɑntɛkst, laɪk wərk. aɪd laɪk tɪ pʊt ˈfɔrwərd ən aɪˈdiə ɪf aɪ may.

VX3iFFXBWdA.text: We could hire a freelancer for the graphics.
VX3iFFXBWdA.ipa: we* kʊd haɪər ə ˈfriˌlænsər fər ðə graphics.

VX3iFFXBWdA.text: Can you dream up or put forward an idea of what word we can look at next time?
VX3iFFXBWdA.ipa: can* ju drim əp ər pʊt ˈfɔrwərd ən aɪˈdiə əv wət wərd wi kən lʊk æt nɛkst time?

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Stress is an ancient superpower that is slowly killing you.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: stress* ɪz ən ˈeɪnʧənt ˌsupərˈpaʊər ðət ɪz sˈloʊli ˈkɪlɪŋ you.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: It evolved as one of the most powerful tools for survival but has turned into
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: it* ɪˈvɑlvd ɛz wən əv ðə moʊst ˈpaʊərfəl tulz fər sərˈvaɪvəl bət həz tərnd into

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: a double edged sword in the modern world. It grants your wish to rise to a gnarly challenge
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: a* ˈdəbəl ɛʤd sɔrd ɪn ðə ˈmɑdərn wərld. ɪt grænts jʊr wɪʃ tɪ raɪz tɪ ə ˈnɑrli challenge

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: or to work harder – but claims a chunk of your life force in return.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: or* tɪ wərk ˈhɑrdər –* bət kleɪmz ə ʧəŋk əv jʊr laɪf fɔrs ɪn return.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Unless you learn how to use it right.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: unless* ju lərn haʊ tɪ juz ɪt right.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: What is stress, why is it harmful and what can you do to escape its consequences?
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: what* ɪz strɛs, waɪ ɪz ɪt ˈhɑrmfəl ənd wət kən ju du tɪ ɪˈskeɪp ɪts consequences?

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: All living things strive for homeostasis, a state where their body is in balance.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: all* ˈlɪvɪŋ θɪŋz straɪv fər ˌhoʊmioʊˈsteɪsəs, ə steɪt wɛr ðɛr ˈbɑdi ɪz ɪn balance.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: To maintain this balance they make adjustments like moving into the sunlight or eating nutrients.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: to* meɪnˈteɪn ðɪs ˈbæləns ðeɪ meɪk əˈʤəstmənts laɪk ˈmuvɪŋ ˈɪntu ðə ˈsənˌlaɪt ər ˈitɪŋ nutrients.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: But the world is a cruel place with death lurking behind every
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: but* ðə wərld ɪz ə kruəl pleɪs wɪθ dɛθ ˈlərkɪŋ bɪˈhaɪnd every

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: corner and organisms need a way to react to extreme situations. This is how stress evolved – as a physical
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: corner* ənd ˈɔrgəˌnɪzəmz nid ə weɪ tɪ riækt tɪ ɪkˈstrim ˌsɪʧuˈeɪʃənz. ðɪs ɪz haʊ strɛs ɪˈvɑlvd –* ɛz ə physical

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: response to a stressor – which is anything challenging or dangerous.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: response* tɪ ə ˈstrɛsər –* wɪʧ ɪz ˈɛniˌθɪŋ ˈʧælənʤɪŋ ər dangerous.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Stress is a very fast, drastic change of the state of the body that prioritizes survival.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: stress* ɪz ə ˈvɛri fæst, ˈdræstɪk ʧeɪnʤ əv ðə steɪt əv ðə ˈbɑdi ðət praɪˈɔrəˌtaɪzɪz survival.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: An emergency program that lets the organism push way out of its comfort zone to survive.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: an* ˈimərʤənsi ˈproʊˌgræm ðət lɛts ðə ˈɔrgəˌnɪzəm pʊʃ weɪ aʊt əv ɪts ˈkəmfərt zoʊn tɪ sərˈvaɪv.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: To understand how stress works in humans, let's visit a scene fifty thousand years ago…
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: tɪ ˌəndərˈstænd haʊ strɛs wərks ɪn ˈjumənz, lɛts ˈvɪzɪt ə sin ˈfɪfti ˈθaʊzənd jɪrz ago…

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: The Superpower Awakens
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: the* ˌsupərˈpaʊər awakens

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: A group of early homo sapiens are huddled around a fire – when suddenly a desperate
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: a* grup əv ˈərli ˈhoʊmoʊ ˈseɪpiənz ər ˈhədəld əraʊnd ə faɪər –* wɪn ˈsədənli ə desperate

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: and starving sabertooth tiger attacks their camp.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: and* ˈstɑrvɪŋ sabertooth* ˈtaɪgər əˈtæks ðɛr camp.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Their leader, Urr the Bearsmasher jumps up as time slows down.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: their* ˈlidər, urr* ðə bearsmasher* ʤəmps əp ɛz taɪm sloʊz down.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: His brain classifies the tiger as a critical stressor and unleashes the stress response.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: his* breɪn ˈklæsəˌfaɪz ðə ˈtaɪgər ɛz ə ˈkrɪtɪkəl ˈstrɛsər ənd ənˈliʃɪz ðə strɛs response.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Adrenal glands pump out adrenaline that rushes through Urr’s body activating “fight or flight” mode.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: adrenal* glændz pəmp aʊt əˈdrɛnələn ðət ˈrəʃɪz θru urr’s* ˈbɑdi ˈæktəˌveɪtɪŋ ““fight* ər flight”*” mode.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: His heart starts beating faster and his lungs accelerate, providing an
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: his* hɑrt stɑrts ˈbitɪŋ ˈfæstər ənd hɪz ləŋz ækˈsɛlərˌeɪt, prəˈvaɪdɪŋ an

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: emergency supply of nutrients and oxygen to all critical systems.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: emergency* səˈplaɪ əv ˈnutriənts ənd ˈɑksɪʤən tɪ ɔl ˈkrɪtɪkəl systems.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Adrenaline dictates that his muscles have energy priority over his other
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: adrenaline* ˈdɪkˌteɪts ðət hɪz ˈməsəlz hæv ˈɛnərʤi praɪˈɔrəti ˈoʊvər hɪz other

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: organs and activates them for rapid action – their strength and speed are ramped up. Pain and fatigue signals are muted,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: organs* ənd ˈæktəˌveɪts ðɛm fər ˈræpɪd ˈækʃən –* ðɛr strɛŋθ ənd spid ər ramped* əp. peɪn ənd fəˈtig ˈsɪgnəlz ər muted,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: while his senses become super sharp and lock in on the threat.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: while* hɪz ˈsɛnsɪz bɪˈkəm ˈsupər ʃɑrp ənd lɑk ɪn ɔn ðə threat.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Urr perceives every detail with maximum clarity and realizes the tiger is getting ready to pounce!
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: urr* pərˈsivz ˈɛvəri ˈditeɪl wɪθ ˈmæksəməm ˈklɛrɪti ənd ˈriəˌlaɪzɪz ðə ˈtaɪgər ɪz ˈgɪtɪŋ ˈrɛdi tɪ pounce!

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: There is only a split second to react. Adrenaline forces Urr to make a quick, impulsive decision:
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: there* ɪz ˈoʊnli ə splɪt ˈsɛkənd tɪ riækt. əˈdrɛnələn ˈfɔrsɪz urr* tɪ meɪk ə kwɪk, ˌɪmˈpəlsɪv dɪˈsɪʒən:

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Without thinking about it he chooses to fight and attacks with full force.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: wɪˈθaʊt ˈθɪŋkɪŋ əˈbaʊt ɪt hi ˈʧuzɪz tɪ faɪt ənd əˈtæks wɪθ fʊl force.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: But the battle is long and arduous – the animal is desperate and will not back down, our humans risk exhaustion.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: but* ðə ˈbætəl ɪz lɔŋ ənd ˈɑrʤuəs –* ðə ˈænəməl ɪz ˈdɛspərɪt ənd wɪl nɑt bæk daʊn, ɑr ˈjumənz rɪsk exhaustion.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: A new hormone joins the battle: cortisol.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: a* nu ˈhɔrˌmoʊn ʤɔɪnz ðə ˈbætəl: cortisol.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Cortisol is the slower, more strategic hormone that manages a longer stress response.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: cortisol* ɪz ðə sloʊər, mɔr strəˈtiʤɪk ˈhɔrˌmoʊn ðət ˈmænɪʤɪz ə ˈlɔŋgər strɛs response.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: It orders a total shift of all body systems. Ramping up the fuel supply while suppressing all non-essential functions like resting,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: it* ˈɔrdərz ə ˈtoʊtəl ʃɪft əv ɔl ˈbɑdi ˈsɪstəmz. ˈræmpɪŋ əp ðə fjuəl səˈplaɪ waɪl səˈprɛsɪŋ ɔl ˌnɑnɪˈsɛnʃəl ˈfəŋkʃənz laɪk resting,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: disease defense or digestion. Urr's body isn’t his own anymore, it belongs to stress.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: disease* dɪˈfɛns ər daɪˈʤɛsʧən. urr's* ˈbɑdi isn’t* hɪz oʊn ˌɛniˈmɔr, ɪt bɪˈlɔŋz tɪ stress.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Finally Urr the Bearsmasher deals the killing blow. The tribe gathers in celebration.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: finally* urr* ðə bearsmasher* dilz ðə ˈkɪlɪŋ bloʊ. ðə traɪb ˈgæðərz ɪn celebration.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Their bodies shut down “fight or flight” and activate “rest and digest”.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: their* ˈbɑdiz ʃət daʊn ““fight* ər flight”*” ənd ˈæktəˌveɪt ““rest* ənd digest”.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Wounds begin to hurt and muscles cry in exhaustion.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: wounds* bɪˈgɪn tɪ hərt ənd ˈməsəlz kraɪ ɪn exhaustion.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: It is time to recover and heal, bond and tell stories about how they killed the angry tiger god.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: it* ɪz taɪm tɪ rɪˈkəvər ənd hil, bɑnd ənd tɛl ˈstɔriz əˈbaʊt haʊ ðeɪ kɪld ðə ˈæŋgri ˈtaɪgər god.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Some fall asleep, others eat to replenish. The tribe relaxes and homeostasis is being restored.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: some* fɔl əsˈlip, ˈəðərz it tɪ riˈplɛnɪʃ. ðə traɪb rɪˈlæksɪz ənd ˌhoʊmioʊˈsteɪsəs ɪz biɪŋ restored.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: These people are your ancestors. When did this amazing superpower turn into a curse?
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: these* ˈpipəl ər jʊr ˈænˌsɛstərz. wɪn dɪd ðɪs əˈmeɪzɪŋ ˌsupərˈpaʊər tərn ˈɪntu ə curse?

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: The Deadly Email Our world has changed so quickly and profoundly that our biology couldn’t keep up.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: the* ˈdɛdli iˈmeɪl ɑr wərld həz ʧeɪnʤd soʊ kˈwɪkli ənd proʊˈfaʊndli ðət ɑr baɪˈɑləʤi couldn’t* kip up.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Stress is still the same it was fifty thousand years ago: Sense a stressor. React immediately and with full force.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: stress* ɪz stɪl ðə seɪm ɪt wɑz ˈfɪfti ˈθaʊzənd jɪrz əˈgoʊ: sɛns ə ˈstrɛsər. riækt ˌɪˈmiˌdiətli ənd wɪθ fʊl force.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Prioritize present moment survival, make sacrifices if necessary.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: prioritize* ˈprɛzənt ˈmoʊmənt sərˈvaɪvəl, meɪk ˈsækrəˌfaɪsɪz ɪf ˈnɛsəˌsɛri.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: That works well when you have to jump out of the way of a car.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: ðət wərks wɛl wɪn ju hæv tɪ ʤəmp aʊt əv ðə weɪ əv ə car.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: But most stressors we encounter nowadays are abstract, acute and more numerous,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: but* moʊst ˈstrɛsərz wi ɪnˈkaʊnər ˈnaʊəˌdeɪz ər ˈæbˌstrækt, əˈkjut ənd mɔr numerous,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: often intangible, persist for much longer and usually don’t even require physical action.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: often* ˌɪnˈtænʤəbəl, pərˈsɪst fər məʧ ˈlɔŋgər ənd ˈjuʒəwəli don’t* ˈivɪn ˌrikˈwaɪər ˈfɪzɪkəl action.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: The tigers of the past are now angry emails, deadlines,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: the* ˈtaɪgərz əv ðə pæst ər naʊ ˈæŋgri iˈmeɪlz, deadlines,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: online dating, rush hour traffic or doom scrolling the news and social media.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: online* ˈdeɪtɪŋ, rəʃ aʊər ˈtræfɪk ər dum scrolling* ðə nuz ənd ˈsoʊʃəl media.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: But our brains are built to take fear very seriously,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: but* ɑr breɪnz ər bɪlt tɪ teɪk fɪr ˈvɛri seriously,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: so if you experience even a short burst of anxiety over an email or
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: so* ɪf ju ɪkˈspɪriəns ˈivɪn ə ʃɔrt bərst əv æŋˈzaɪəti ˈoʊvər ən iˈmeɪl or

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: because you scrolled through twitter, the helping hand of stress springs to action.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: because* ju scrolled* θru tˈwɪtər, ðə ˈhɛlpɪŋ hænd əv strɛs spərɪŋz tɪ action.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: You can have dozens of these micro stressors per day constantly activating your stress response, even if you barely notice anymore.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: you* kən hæv ˈdəzənz əv ðiz ˈmaɪˌkroʊ ˈstrɛsərz pər deɪ ˈkɑnstəntli ˈæktəˌveɪtɪŋ jʊr strɛs rɪˈspɑns, ˈivɪn ɪf ju ˈbɛrli ˈnoʊtɪs anymore.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: While these micro-threats stack up we also have to deal with bigger stressors like unpaid overtime, angry clients, rising rents,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: while* ðiz micro-threats* stæk əp wi ˈɔlsoʊ hæv tɪ dil wɪθ ˈbɪgər ˈstrɛsərz laɪk ənˈpeɪd ˈoʊvərˌtaɪm, ˈæŋgri klaɪənts, ˈraɪzɪŋ rents,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: inflation and healthcare or tariffs and chaos making our future more uncertain.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: inflation* ənd ˈhɛlθˌkɛr ər ˈtɛrəfs ənd keɪɑs ˈmeɪkɪŋ ɑr fˈjuʧər mɔr uncertain.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: But the boost we get from stress can also be extremely helpful if
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: but* ðə bust wi gɪt frəm strɛs kən ˈɔlsoʊ bi ɪkˈstrimli ˈhɛlpfəl if

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: you have to study for an exam, run a marathon or complete a big project.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: you* hæv tɪ ˈstədi fər ən ɪgˈzæm, rən ə ˈmɛrəˌθɑn ər kəmˈplit ə bɪg project.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: It is a proper superpower and can feel exhilarating, even addictive. So many of us end up abusing it.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: it* ɪz ə ˈprɑpər ˌsupərˈpaʊər ənd kən fil ɪgˈzɪlərˌeɪtɪŋ, ˈivɪn əˈdɪktɪv. soʊ ˈmɛni əv ˈjuˈɛs ɛnd əp əˈbjuzɪŋ it.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Especially if you work in a competitive field you may keep pushing and challenging
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: especially* ɪf ju wərk ɪn ə kəmˈpɛtɪtɪv fild ju meɪ kip ˈpʊʃɪŋ ənd ˈʧælənʤɪŋ

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: yourselves and others, relying on stress to get your best performance possible. The problem is that stress was only ever intended to
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: ˈjɔrsɛlvz ənd ˈəðərz, riˈlaɪɪŋ ɔn strɛs tɪ gɪt jʊr bɛst pərˈfɔrməns ˈpɑsəbəl. ðə ˈprɑbləm ɪz ðət strɛs wɑz ˈoʊnli ˈɛvər ˌɪnˈtɛndɪd to

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: be used in short spurts and critical situations. If it is activated over days, weeks and months with no release,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: be* juzd ɪn ʃɔrt spərts ənd ˈkrɪtɪkəl ˌsɪʧuˈeɪʃənz. ɪf ɪt ɪz ˈæktɪˌveɪtɪd ˈoʊvər deɪz, wiks ənd mənθs wɪθ noʊ release,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: it becomes chronic stress, one of the deadliest conditions for humans.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: it* bɪˈkəmz ˈkrɑnɪk strɛs, wən əv ðə ˈdɛdliəst kənˈdɪʃənz fər humans.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: How Chronic Stress Destroys You
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: how* ˈkrɑnɪk strɛs dɪˈstrɔɪz you

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: The price of chronic stress is the slow destruction of every part of your body and brain.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: the* praɪs əv ˈkrɑnɪk strɛs ɪz ðə sloʊ dɪˈstrəkʃən əv ˈɛvəri pɑrt əv jʊr ˈbɑdi ənd brain.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: As the stress cascade is activated over and over, adrenaline and cortisol keep flooding your body.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: as* ðə strɛs kæˈskeɪd ɪz ˈæktɪˌveɪtɪd ˈoʊvər ənd ˈoʊvər, əˈdrɛnələn ənd ˈkɔrtɪˌsɔl kip ˈflədɪŋ jʊr body.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Your „fight or flight“ systems are overactivated while your „rest and digest“ functions are constantly suppressed.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: your* „„fight* ər flight“*“ ˈsɪstəmz ər overactivated* waɪl jʊr „„rest* ənd digest“*“ ˈfəŋkʃənz ər ˈkɑnstəntli suppressed.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Your muscles tense up for a fight that never comes – instead your back,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: your* ˈməsəlz tɛns əp fər ə faɪt ðət ˈnɛvər kəmz –* ˌɪnˈstɛd jʊr back,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: shoulders and neck scream under the tension and you get pounding headaches.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: shoulders* ənd nɛk skrim ˈəndər ðə ˈtɛnʃən ənd ju gɪt ˈpaʊndɪŋ headaches.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Your digestive tract cramps and suffers, either holding things in too long or rushing things out.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: your* daɪˈʤɛstɪv trækt kræmps ənd ˈsəfərz, ˈiðər ˈhoʊldɪŋ θɪŋz ɪn tu lɔŋ ər ˈrəʃɪŋ θɪŋz out.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Your fat metabolism is disrupted and starts slowing down fat breakdown, while increasing fat build up in your belly area.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: your* fæt məˈtæbəˌlɪzəm ɪz dɪsˈrəptɪd ənd stɑrts sloʊɪŋ daʊn fæt ˈbreɪkˌdaʊn, waɪl ˌɪnˈkrisɪŋ fæt bɪld əp ɪn jʊr ˈbɛli area.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Your cells become less responsive to insulin increasing your risk of metabolic diseases.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: your* sɛlz bɪˈkəm lɛs rɪˈspɑnsɪv tɪ ˈɪnsələn ˌɪnˈkrisɪŋ jʊr rɪsk əv ˌmɛtəˈbɑlɪk diseases.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Your heart keeps pounding, eager to supply your body with fuel but your
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: jʊr hɑrt kips ˈpaʊndɪŋ, ˈigər tɪ səˈplaɪ jʊr ˈbɑdi wɪθ fjuəl bət your

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: blood vessels aren’t built for this constant overactivation and get destroyed in the process,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: blood* ˈvɛsəlz aren’t* bɪlt fər ðɪs ˈkɑnstənt overactivation* ənd gɪt dɪˈstrɔɪd ɪn ðə process,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: massively increasing your risk of a heart attack or stroke. Your immune system gets weaker and stronger at the same time,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: massively* ˌɪnˈkrisɪŋ jʊr rɪsk əv ə hɑrt əˈtæk ər stroʊk. jʊr ˌɪmˈjun ˈsɪstəm gɪts ˈwikər ənd ˈstrɔŋgər æt ðə seɪm time,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: in a way that causes maximum damage to you. The parts that protect you from disease are suppressed
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: in* ə weɪ ðət ˈkɔzɪz ˈmæksəməm ˈdæmɪʤ tɪ ju. ðə pɑrts ðət prəˈtɛkt ju frəm dɪˈziz ər suppressed

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: because who cares about a cold if you have to fight a tiger?
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: because* hu kɛrz əˈbaʊt ə koʊld ɪf ju hæv tɪ faɪt ə ˈtaɪgər?

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: This increases your risk of infections, cancer and autoimmune diseases.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: ðɪs ˌɪnˈkrisɪz jʊr rɪsk əv ˌɪnˈfɛkʃənz, ˈkænsər ənd ˌɔˈtɔɪmjun diseases.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Meanwhile cells that cause inflammation for wound healing are massively boosted, inflaming your tissue all over.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: meanwhile* sɛlz ðət kɔz ˌɪnfləˈmeɪʃən fər wund ˈhilɪŋ ər ˈmæsɪvli ˈbustɪd, ɪnˈfleɪmɪŋ jʊr ˈtɪʃu ɔl over.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Your brain gets rewired to serve stress. Perceiving an ongoing critical emergency it slows down calm reflection and thinking.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: your* breɪn gɪts riˈwaɪərd tɪ sərv strɛs. pərˈsivɪŋ ən ˈɔnˌgoʊɪŋ ˈkrɪtɪkəl ˈimərʤənsi ɪt sloʊz daʊn kɑm rɪˈflɛkʃən ənd thinking.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Instead favoring quick and rapid reactions. You literally get worse at thinking clearly and lose wisdom points.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: instead* ˈfeɪvərɪŋ kwɪk ənd ˈræpɪd riˈækʃənz. ju ˈlɪtərəli gɪt wərs æt ˈθɪŋkɪŋ ˈklɪrli ənd luz ˈwɪzdəm points.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: You are much more likely to make bad, impulsive decisions like breaking up, eating junk food, or using drugs.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: you* ər məʧ mɔr ˈlaɪkli tɪ meɪk bæd, ˌɪmˈpəlsɪv dɪˈsɪʒənz laɪk ˈbreɪkɪŋ əp, ˈitɪŋ ʤəŋk fud, ər ˈjuzɪŋ drugs.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: You become worse at solving your problems while creating new ones.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: you* bɪˈkəm wərs æt ˈsɑlvɪŋ jʊr ˈprɑbləmz waɪl kriˈeɪtɪŋ nu ones.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Under chronic stress you are simply a worse version of yourself. If this persists your memory and cognitive abilities get
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: under* ˈkrɑnɪk strɛs ju ər ˈsɪmpli ə wərs ˈvərʒən əv ˈjɔrsɛlf. ɪf ðɪs pərˈsɪsts jʊr ˈmɛməri ənd ˈkɑgnɪtɪv əˈbɪləˌtiz get

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: worse as you slide into anxiety and depression. So your chances of getting out of a stressful situation in the
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: worse* ɛz ju slaɪd ˈɪntu æŋˈzaɪəti ənd dɪˈprɛʃən. soʊ jʊr ˈʧænsɪz əv ˈgɪtɪŋ aʊt əv ə ˈstrɛsfəl ˌsɪʧuˈeɪʃən ɪn the

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: modern world worsen the longer you are chronically stressed. A hellish, vicious circle.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: modern* wərld ˈwərsən ðə ˈlɔŋgər ju ər ˈkrɑnɪkəli strɛst. ə ˈhɛlɪʃ, ˈvɪʃəs circle.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: If you can’t escape it, odds are that you may die earlier and suffer greatly along the way.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: if* ju can’t* ɪˈskeɪp ɪt, ɑdz ər ðət ju meɪ daɪ ˈərliər ənd ˈsəfər ˈgreɪtli əˈlɔŋ ðə way.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: How to use Stress As Intended Stress can be your friend.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: how* tɪ juz strɛs ɛz ˌɪnˈtɛndɪd strɛs kən bi jʊr friend.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: In measured doses, acute stress enables you to conquer mountains and push beyond your
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: in* ˈmɛʒərd ˈdoʊsɪz, əˈkjut strɛs ɪˈneɪbəlz ju tɪ ˈkɑŋkər ˈmaʊntənz ənd pʊʃ bɪɔnd your

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: comfort zone, helps you to improve your skills and grow as a person.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: comfort* zoʊn, hɛlps ju tɪ ˌɪmˈpruv jʊr skɪlz ənd groʊ ɛz ə person.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: And it can make it feel amazing to perform at your very best. But to get the good without the bad,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: and* ɪt kən meɪk ɪt fil əˈmeɪzɪŋ tɪ pərˈfɔrm æt jʊr ˈvɛri bɛst. bət tɪ gɪt ðə gʊd wɪˈθaʊt ðə bad,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: it’s critical that you properly wind down your flight or flight response and relax. Listen to your body.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: it’s* ˈkrɪtɪkəl ðət ju ˈprɑpərli wɪnd daʊn jʊr flaɪt ər flaɪt rɪˈspɑns ənd rɪˈlæks. ˈlɪsən tɪ jʊr ˈbɑdi.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: If you feel stressed, you are stressed, full stop.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: ɪf ju fil strɛst, ju ər strɛst, fʊl stop.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: There is no universal threshold you have to reach before you’re allowed to do something about it. Next, ask yourself:
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: there* ɪz noʊ ˌjunəˈvərsəl θˈrɛˌʃoʊld ju hæv tɪ riʧ ˌbiˈfɔr you’re* əˈlaʊd tɪ du ˈsəmθɪŋ əˈbaʊt ɪt. nɛkst, æsk yourself:

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Is your stress response productive in your situation and is there an end in sight?
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: is* jʊr strɛs rɪˈspɑns pərˈdəktɪv ɪn jʊr ˌsɪʧuˈeɪʃən ənd ɪz ðɛr ən ɛnd ɪn sight?

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: If you are studying for exams or moving house stress is a superpower, helping you unlock your full potential.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: if* ju ər ˈstədiɪŋ fər ɪgˈzæmz ər ˈmuvɪŋ haʊs strɛs ɪz ə ˌsupərˈpaʊər, ˈhɛlpɪŋ ju ənˈlɑk jʊr fʊl potential.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: But your health needs breaks from “fight or flight” mode.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: but* jʊr hɛlθ nidz breɪks frəm ““fight* ər flight”*” mode.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Be it meeting friends, working out or going for a walk in nature.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: be* ɪt ˈmitɪŋ frɛndz, ˈwərkɪŋ aʊt ər goʊɪŋ fər ə wɔk ɪn nature.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Relaxation is not being lazy, this is serious self care.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: relaxation* ɪz nɑt biɪŋ ˈleɪzi, ðɪs ɪz ˈsɪriəs sɛlf care.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: If you relax actively and regularly you will keep stress from claiming a
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: if* ju rɪˈlæks ˈæktɪvli ənd ˈrɛgjələrli ju wɪl kip strɛs frəm ˈkleɪmɪŋ a

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: price on your body and mind and still be able to use its power.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: price* ɔn jʊr ˈbɑdi ənd maɪnd ənd stɪl bi ˈeɪbəl tɪ juz ɪts power.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: If you can’t do this at the moment, plan active relaxation time as soon as you can. Without this stressing you out.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: if* ju can’t* du ðɪs æt ðə ˈmoʊmənt, plæn ˈæktɪv ˌrilækˈseɪʃən taɪm ɛz sun ɛz ju kən. wɪˈθaʊt ðɪs ˈstrɛsɪŋ ju out.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Ok, but what if you suffer from constant unhelpful stress?
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: ok*, bət wət ɪf ju ˈsəfər frəm ˈkɑnstənt ənˈhɛlpfəl stress?

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: You can’t control your body’s stress response, but you can influence it. As annoying as this is:
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: you* can’t* kənˈtroʊl jʊr body’s* strɛs rɪˈspɑns, bət ju kən ˈɪnfluəns ɪt. ɛz əˈnɔɪɪŋ ɛz ðɪs is:

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: mindfulness, like meditation or yoga, is scientifically proven to take the
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: mindfulness*, laɪk ˌmɛdəˈteɪʃən ər ˈjoʊgə, ɪz ˌsaɪənˈtɪfɪkəli ˈpruvən tɪ teɪk the

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: edge off stressful situations and to tell your brain that this email is not a deadly tiger.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: edge* ɔf ˈstrɛsfəl ˌsɪʧuˈeɪʃənz ənd tɪ tɛl jʊr breɪn ðət ðɪs iˈmeɪl ɪz nɑt ə ˈdɛdli tiger.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: But the single most effective way to remove stress from your life, is to remove the stressor itself.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: but* ðə ˈsɪŋgəl moʊst ˈifɛktɪv weɪ tɪ riˈmuv strɛs frəm jʊr laɪf, ɪz tɪ riˈmuv ðə ˈstrɛsər ˌɪtˈsɛlf.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Become your own personal assistant and assess where tiny bursts of stress are coming from during your day.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: bɪˈkəm jʊr oʊn ˈpərsɪnəl əˈsɪstənt ənd əˈsɛs wɛr ˈtaɪni bərsts əv strɛs ər ˈkəmɪŋ frəm ˈdʊrɪŋ jʊr day.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Do you feel stressed when you doom scroll? Social media doesn’t show us what we want, but what makes us engage.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: do* ju fil strɛst wɪn ju dum skroʊl? ˈsoʊʃəl ˈmidiə doesn’t* ʃoʊ ˈjuˈɛs wət wi wɔnt, bət wət meɪks ˈjuˈɛs engage.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: And negative emotions make us engage the most. So simply getting rid of the apps or unfollowing stressful people can
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: and* ˈnɛgətɪv ˈɪˌmoʊʃənz meɪk ˈjuˈɛs ɪnˈgeɪʤ ðə moʊst. soʊ ˈsɪmpli ˈgɪtɪŋ rɪd əv ðə æps ər unfollowing* ˈstrɛsfəl ˈpipəl can

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: actually remove a lot of stress from your life. Especially with the really big problems, removing the stressor is extremely effective.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: actually* riˈmuv ə lɔt əv strɛs frəm jʊr laɪf. əˈspɛʃəli wɪθ ðə ˈrɪli bɪg ˈprɑbləmz, riˈmuvɪŋ ðə ˈstrɛsər ɪz ɪkˈstrimli effective.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: If you live in a dangerous neighborhood: move. If you are bullied at school: see if you can change your environment.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: if* ju lɪv ɪn ə ˈdeɪnʤərəs ˈneɪbərˌhʊd: muv. ɪf ju ər ˈbʊlid æt skul: si ɪf ju kən ʧeɪnʤ jʊr environment.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: While this is drastic advice and may not be easy, it will have the biggest impact on your chronic stress levels.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: while* ðɪs ɪz ˈdræstɪk ədˈvaɪs ənd meɪ nɑt bi ˈizi, ɪt wɪl hæv ðə ˈbɪgəst ˌɪmˈpækt ɔn jʊr ˈkrɑnɪk strɛs levels.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: So think about what you can change in the mid term. Of course there are situations where you can’t just eliminate
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: so* θɪŋk əˈbaʊt wət ju kən ʧeɪnʤ ɪn ðə mɪd tərm. əv kɔrs ðɛr ər ˌsɪʧuˈeɪʃənz wɛr ju can’t* ʤɪst eliminate

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: the stressor or meditate the chronic stress away.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: the* ˈstrɛsər ər ˈmɛdəˌteɪt ðə ˈkrɑnɪk strɛs away.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: If your brain is stuck in stress mode for too long it will get worse and worse at solving problems.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: if* jʊr breɪn ɪz stək ɪn strɛs moʊd fər tu lɔŋ ɪt wɪl gɪt wərs ənd wərs æt ˈsɑlvɪŋ problems.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: You may not be able to think clearly to get out of your situation.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: you* meɪ nɑt bi ˈeɪbəl tɪ θɪŋk ˈklɪrli tɪ gɪt aʊt əv jʊr situation.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: In these cases the most powerful move is to ask for help. Open up to friends or family or get professional help.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: in* ðiz ˈkeɪsɪz ðə moʊst ˈpaʊərfəl muv ɪz tɪ æsk fər hɛlp. ˈoʊpən əp tɪ frɛndz ər ˈfæməli ər gɪt prəˈfɛʃənəl help.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: An extra pair of hands can make a huge difference. In the end, life will throw stressors your way, this is unavoidable.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: an* ˈɛkstrə pɛr əv hænz kən meɪk ə juʤ ˈdɪfərəns. ɪn ðə ɛnd, laɪf wɪl θroʊ ˈstrɛsərz jʊr weɪ, ðɪs ɪz unavoidable.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: And that’s ok. Stress is your superpower. Use it. But never forget to get to the other side of it.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: and* that’s* ˈoʊˈkeɪ. strɛs ɪz jʊr ˌsupərˈpaʊər. juz ɪt. bət ˈnɛvər fərˈgɛt tɪ gɪt tɪ ðə ˈəðər saɪd əv it.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: That’s why we created a Health Journal. We’ve collected all the latest health science and designed a framework to help
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: that’s* waɪ wi kriˈeɪtɪd ə hɛlθ ˈʤərnəl. we’ve* kəˈlɛktəd ɔl ðə ˈleɪtəst hɛlθ saɪəns ənd dɪˈzaɪnd ə ˈfreɪmˌwərk tɪ help

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: you figure out what actually works for your life – without pressure or guilt.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: you* ˈfɪgjər aʊt wət ˈæˌkʧuəli wərks fər jʊr laɪf –* wɪˈθaʊt ˈprɛʃər ər guilt.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: If you want to reduce stress, start with the Rest & Mental Health chapter. Or dive into Nutrition or Physical Fitness.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: and inspiration to get you started – but you can make it completely your own to fit your needs.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: In the end, you’ll have built a custom health mix that works for you.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: We also made it as beautiful and fun as possible (as taking care of your health should always be).

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: And you’re not doing this alone! We’ll check in from time to time, and we’d love to hear from you:

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: share your progress and inspire other birbs on their health journey. After the Gratitude Journal and the Habit Journal,
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: share* jʊr ˈprɑˌgrɛs ənd ˌɪnˈspaɪr ˈəðər birbs* ɔn ðɛr hɛlθ ˈʤərni. ˈæftər ðə ˈgrætəˌtud ˈʤərnəl ənd ðə ˈhæbət journal,

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: this is the third part in our bestselling journal series.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: this* ɪz ðə θərd pɑrt ɪn ɑr ˈbɛˈsɛlɪŋ ˈʤərnəl series.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: To kick-start your health journey, we’ve also created some exclusive Health Club Merch for you.
Mo1A45ShcMo.ipa: to* kick-start* jʊr hɛlθ ˈʤərni, we’ve* ˈɔlsoʊ kriˈeɪtɪd səm ɪkˈsklusɪv hɛlθ kləb merch* fər you.

Mo1A45ShcMo.text: Wear them as companions to remind you of your goal. Just give yourself one month. Try it. See where it takes you.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: 6 Minute English. From BBC Learning English.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: 6 ˈmɪnət ˈɪŋlɪʃ. frəm ˌbibiˈsi ˈlərnɪŋ english.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Hello, I'm Rob. Welcome to 6 Minute English. With me in the studio is Neil. — Hello, Neil. — Hello, Rob.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: hello*, əm rɑb. ˈwɛlkəm tɪ 6 ˈmɪnət ˈɪŋlɪʃ. wɪθ mi ɪn ðə ˈstudiˌoʊ ɪz nil. —* hɛˈloʊ, nil. —* hɛˈloʊ, rob.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: And in this programme we're talking about tourism, but in a very special place — Antarctica.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: and* ɪn ðɪs ˈproʊˌgræm wɪr ˈtɔkɪŋ əˈbaʊt ˈtʊˌrɪzəm, bət ɪn ə ˈvɛri ˈspɛʃəl pleɪs —* antarctica.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: It's considered the last great wilderness on Earth. 'Wilderness' means 'an area with no people and no agriculture
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: it's* kənˈsɪdərd ðə læst greɪt ˈwɪldərnəs ɔn ərθ. 'ˈwɪldərnəs' minz 'ən ˈɛriə wɪθ noʊ ˈpipəl ənd noʊ agriculture

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: 'because of the difficult living conditions'. Yes, in Antarctica there are only research stations with scientists and a few tourists.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: 'because* əv ðə ˈdɪfəkəlt ˈlɪvɪŋ kənˈdɪʃənz'. jɛs, ɪn ˌæˈnɑrtɪkə ðɛr ər ˈoʊnli ˈrisərʧ ˈsteɪʃənz wɪθ ˈsaɪəntɪsts ənd ə fju tourists.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Well, not so few — about 37,000 tourists are expected there this season.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: well*, nɑt soʊ fju —* əˈbaʊt 37,000* ˈtʊrɪsts ər ɪkˈspɛktɪd ðɛr ðɪs season.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Many don't go ashore, but there's no denying that it disturbs the environment. — That many? — Yes.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: isn't* ɪt taɪm tɪ rɪˈvil ðə ˈænsər tɪ jʊr kˈwɛʃən, nil? raɪt. aɪ æst ju wɪʧ ˌɪˈtæljən food

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: We're asking if it's fair for tourists 'to set foot' — it means 'to go to' — such a sensitive environment.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: we're* ˈæskɪŋ ɪf ɪts fɛr fər ˈtʊrɪsts 'tɪ sɛt fʊt' —* ɪt minz 'tɪ goʊ tɪ' —* səʧ ə ˈsɛnsɪtɪv environment.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: We'll also use some vocabulary related to Antarctica. By the way, Neil, do you know a lot about the South Pole?
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: wɪl ˈɔlsoʊ juz səm voʊˈkæbjəˌlɛri rɪˈleɪtɪd tɪ ˌæˈnɑrtɪkə. baɪ ðə weɪ, nil, du ju noʊ ə lɔt əˈbaʊt ðə saʊθ pole?

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: I've been reading that the 'ice caps' — these are 'the thick layers of ice
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: i've* bɪn ˈrɛdɪŋ ðət ðə 'aɪs kæps' —* ðiz ər 'ðə θɪk leɪərz əv ice

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: 'permanently covering a vast area of land in the Arctic and Antarctic' — are melting, due to global warming.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: 'permanently* ˈkəvərɪŋ ə væst ˈɛriə əv lænd ɪn ðə ˈɑrtɪk ənd æˈnɑrtɪk' —* ər ˈmɛltɪŋ, du tɪ ˈgloʊbəl warming.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Yes, and 'global warming' is 'the increase in world temperatures 'due to the presence of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere'.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: yes*, ənd 'ˈgloʊbəl ˈwɔrmɪŋ' ɪz 'ðə ˌɪnˈkris ɪn wərld ˈtɛmpərəʧərz 'du tɪ ðə ˈprɛzəns əv ˈkɑrbən daɪˈɑkˌsaɪd ɪn ðə atmosphere'.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: This gas and some others have been stopping heat from the Earth escaping into space.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: this* gæs ənd səm ˈəðərz hæv bɪn ˈstɑpɪŋ hit frəm ðə ərθ ɪˈskeɪpɪŋ ˈɪntu space.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: You know what, Rob? I would like to visit Antarctica before it melts too much.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: ju noʊ wət, rɑb? aɪ wʊd laɪk tɪ ˈvɪzɪt ˌæˈnɑrtɪkə ˌbiˈfɔr ɪt mɛlts tu much.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: I want to see the penguins. They're very amusing animals!
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: i* wɔnt tɪ si ðə ˈpɛŋgwənz. ðɛr ˈvɛri əmˈjuzɪŋ ˈænəməlz!

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: They are, yes. But penguins aside, what large resource can be found in Antarctica? That's my question for you today.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: they* ər, jɛs. bət ˈpɛŋgwənz əˈsaɪd, wət lɑrʤ ˈrisɔrs kən bi faʊnd ɪn ˌæˈnɑrtɪkə? ðæts maɪ kˈwɛʃən fər ju today.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Is Antarctica a) The world's largest coal field?
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: is* ˌæˈnɑrtɪkə ə) ðə wərldz ˈlɑrʤəst koʊl field?

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: b) The world's largest gold source? c) The world's largest diamond source?
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: b*) ðə wərldz ˈlɑrʤəst goʊld sɔrs? si) ðə wərldz ˈlɑrʤəst ˈdaɪmənd source?

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: I'm gonna have a guess, cos I don't know, that it's coal, a). Coal. Right, OK.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: i'm* ˈgɑnə hæv ə gɛs, kɔs aɪ doʊnt noʊ, ðət ɪts koʊl, ə). koʊl. raɪt, ok.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Well, as usual, we'll give you the answer at the end of the programme. Well, I love travelling, but I wonder
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: well*, ɛz ˈjuʒəwəl, wɪl gɪv ju ðə ˈænsər æt ðə ɛnd əv ðə ˈproʊˌgræm. wɛl, aɪ ləv ˈtrævəlɪŋ, bət aɪ wonder

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: how that very sensitive environment in Antarctica is going to be preserved.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: how* ðət ˈvɛri ˈsɛnsɪtɪv ɪnˈvaɪrənmənt ɪn ˌæˈnɑrtɪkə ɪz goʊɪŋ tɪ bi preserved.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: That's why BBC reporter Juliet Rix's visit to Antarctica caught my attention. I bet she's asking the same question as you, Rob.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: that's* waɪ ˌbibiˈsi rɪˈpɔrtər ˈʤuliˌɛt rix's* ˈvɪzɪt tɪ ˌæˈnɑrtɪkə kɔt maɪ əˈtɛnʃən. aɪ bɛt ʃiz ˈæskɪŋ ðə seɪm kˈwɛʃən ɛz ju, rob.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Yes, she is. Listen to what she has to say about the need to have some level of tourism in the Antarctic.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: yes*, ʃi ɪz. ˈlɪsən tɪ wət ʃi həz tɪ seɪ əˈbaʊt ðə nid tɪ hæv səm ˈlɛvəl əv ˈtʊˌrɪzəm ɪn ðə antarctic.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: What word does she use to describe people who defend a cause — in this case, the preservation of the region?
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: what* wərd dɪz ʃi juz tɪ dɪˈskraɪb ˈpipəl hu dɪˈfɛnd ə kɔz —* ɪn ðɪs keɪs, ðə ˌprɛzərˈveɪʃən əv ðə region?

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: I'm all too aware that this is not my habitat.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: i'm* ɔl tu əˈwɛr ðət ðɪs ɪz nɑt maɪ habitat.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Like a scuba diver under the sea, I'm an alien visitor in the penguins' world.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: like* ə ˈskubə ˈdaɪvər ˈəndər ðə si, əm ən ˈeɪliən ˈvɪzɪtər ɪn ðə ˈpɛŋgwənz' world.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Which makes me wonder, should I be here at all? Am I, just by setting foot on this extraordinary continent,
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: which* meɪks mi ˈwəndər, ʃʊd aɪ bi hir æt ɔl? æm aɪ, ʤɪst baɪ ˈsɛtɪŋ fʊt ɔn ðɪs ˌɛkstrəˈɔrdəˌnɛri continent,

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: polluting the last great wilderness on Earth? All visitors leave a footprint, admits my tour leader.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: polluting* ðə læst greɪt ˈwɪldərnəs ɔn ərθ? ɔl ˈvɪzɪtərz liv ə ˈfʊtˌprɪnt, ədˈmɪts maɪ tʊr leader.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: And we all go to the same places, the accessible coastline, which is also where the penguins and seals go to breed.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: and* wi ɔl goʊ tɪ ðə seɪm ˈpleɪsɪz, ðə ækˈsɛsəbəl ˈkoʊˌstlaɪn, wɪʧ ɪz ˈɔlsoʊ wɛr ðə ˈpɛŋgwənz ənd silz goʊ tɪ brid.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Nonetheless, he argues, carefully controlled, tourism is not just OK but useful.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: ˌnənðəˈlɛs, hi ˈɑrgjuz, ˈkɛrfəli kənˈtroʊld, ˈtʊˌrɪzəm ɪz nɑt ʤɪst ˈoʊˈkeɪ bət useful.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Without a native population of its own, Antarctica needs advocates. And tourism creates a global constituency
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: without* ə ˈneɪtɪv ˌpɑpjəˈleɪʃən əv ɪts oʊn, ˌæˈnɑrtɪkə nidz ˈædvəˌkeɪts. ənd ˈtʊˌrɪzəm kriˈeɪts ə ˈgloʊbəl constituency

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: of people ready to support, and indeed fund, its preservation. Not everyone is convinced that the benefits outweigh the risks,
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: of* ˈpipəl ˈrɛdi tɪ səˈpɔrt, ənd ˌɪnˈdid fənd, ɪts ˌprɛzərˈveɪʃən. nɑt ˈɛvriˌwən ɪz kənˈvɪnst ðət ðə ˈbɛnəfɪts ˈaʊtˌweɪ ðə risks,

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: but most are pragmatic. The reporter uses the word 'advocates' —
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: but* moʊst ər prægˈmætɪk. ðə rɪˈpɔrtər ˈjuzɪz ðə wərd 'ˈædvəˌkeɪts' —

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: that's what we call 'people who defend a cause or an idea'.
Juliet Rix's tour guide told her it's good that some people go to Antarctica
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: that's* wət wi kɔl 'ˈpipəl hu dɪˈfɛnd ə kɔz ər ən idea'.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: and then, when they go back to their countries, they defend conservation
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: juliet* rix's* tʊr gaɪd toʊld hər ɪts gʊd ðət səm ˈpipəl goʊ tɪ antarctica

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: and give money to organisations which work for the preservation of the environment.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: and* ðɛn, wɪn ðeɪ goʊ bæk tɪ ðɛr ˈkəntriz, ðeɪ dɪˈfɛnd conservation

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Some people might not agree because, if there are some companies making profit,
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: and* gɪv ˈməni tɪ ˌɔrgənɪˈzeɪʃənz wɪʧ wərk fər ðə ˌprɛzərˈveɪʃən əv ðə environment.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: it might be difficult to prevent an increase in tourism to Antarctica. And what control do they have over the tourists?
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: some* ˈpipəl maɪt nɑt əˈgri bɪˈkəz, ɪf ðɛr ər səm ˈkəmpəˌniz ˈmeɪkɪŋ profit,

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Juliet Rix tells us about the instructions given to her group, when they approached Antarctica.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: juliet* rɪks tɛlz ˈjuˈɛs əˈbaʊt ðə ˌɪnˈstrəkʃənz ˈgɪvɪn tɪ hər grup, wɪn ðeɪ əˈproʊʧt antarctica.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: She says that tourists must clean their clothes with a vacuum cleaner,
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: juliet* rɪks tɛlz ˈjuˈɛs əˈbaʊt ðə ˌɪnˈstrəkʃənz ˈgɪvɪn tɪ hər grup, wɪn ðeɪ əˈproʊʧt antarctica.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: before they leave the ship to go on land. But why?
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: she* sɪz ðət ˈtʊrɪsts məst klin ðɛr kloʊðz wɪθ ə ˈvækjum cleaner,

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: We're given a mandatory briefing before gathering for a “vacuum party”. We bio secure ourselves,
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: we're* ˈgɪvɪn ə ˈmændəˌtɔri ˈbrifɪŋ ˌbiˈfɔr ˈgæðərɪŋ fər ə ““vacuum* party”*”. wi baɪoʊ sɪˈkjʊr ourselves,

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: hoovering our clothes and kit and disinfecting our boots, to ensure we introduce no alien species to Antarctica.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: we're* ˈgɪvɪn ə ˈmændəˌtɔri ˈbrifɪŋ ˌbiˈfɔr ˈgæðərɪŋ fər ə ““vacuum* party”*”. wi baɪoʊ sɪˈkjʊr ourselves,

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: There's no eating or smoking on land, and we're instructed to take nothing away, except photographs, and leave nothing behind,
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: ðɛrz noʊ ˈitɪŋ ər sˈmoʊkɪŋ ɔn lænd, ənd wɪr ˌɪnˈstrəktɪd tɪ teɪk ˈnəθɪŋ əˈweɪ, ɪkˈsɛpt ˈfoʊtəˌgræfs, ənd liv ˈnəθɪŋ behind,

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: not even a bit of yellow snow, so don't drink too much at breakfast!
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: not* ˈivɪn ə bɪt əv ˈjɛloʊ snoʊ, soʊ doʊnt drɪŋk tu məʧ æt breakfast!

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: The BBC reporter tells us that the group of tourists has to disinfect their boots. 'Disinfect' means 'to clean something using chemicals'
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: the* ˌbibiˈsi rɪˈpɔrtər tɛlz ˈjuˈɛs ðət ðə grup əv ˈtʊrɪsts həz tɪ dɪsɪnˈfɛkt ðɛr buts. 'dɪsɪnˈfɛkt' minz 'tɪ klin ˈsəmθɪŋ ˈjuzɪŋ chemicals'

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: or, in this case, vacuum to kill or remove bacteria. This is to avoid the risk of contaminating the region.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: or*, ɪn ðɪs keɪs, ˈvækjum tɪ kɪl ər riˈmuv bækˈtɪriə. ðɪs ɪz tɪ əˈvɔɪd ðə rɪsk əv kənˈtæməˌneɪtɪŋ ðə region.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: And to go to the toilet before leaving the ship. The ice is not your toilet!

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: No, it isn't. The penguins have exclusive rights on that! But what do you think about visiting Antarctica, Rob?

from various threats and in need of protection. 'Heritage' means 'the things a society considers important
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: tramping* əraʊnd, ˈdrɑpɪŋ ˈlɪtər, ˈskrɪbəlɪŋ grəˈfiti ˈɛvriˌwɛr. aɪ wʊd ˈnɛvər drɔp ˈlɪtər ər ˈskrɪbəl graffiti!

OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: but* lɛts ˈlɪsən naʊ tɪ ˌbibiˈsi rɪˈpɔrtər ˈrɑʤər harrabin* ˈtɔkɪŋ əˈbaʊt ˈəðər taɪps əv θrɛt tɪ ˈhɛrɪtɪʤ sites.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: the* ˈbɛriər rif əv bɛˈliz ɪn saʊθ əˈmɛrɪkə, ˈsɛkənd ˈbɪgəst ɔn ərθ —* ɔl ˈfeɪsɪŋ θrɛts frəm humans.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: The Great Barrier Reef is attracting urgent concern. There's a huge battle over mining and port development.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: that's* ðə ˌbibiˈsis ˈrɑʤər harrabin*. wɛl, aɪ ˈspɑtɪd ə ˈkəpəl əv θrɛts tɪ ˈhɛrɪtɪʤ saɪts ðɛr —* ˈmaɪnɪŋ ənd pɔrt development.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: mm*. 'ˈmaɪnɪŋ' ɪz 'ðə ˈprɔˌsɛs əv ɛkˈstræktɪŋ koʊl ər ˈəðər ˈmɪnərəlz frəm ðə graʊnd',

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: and if you 'encroach on something', it means you 'move beyond acceptable limits'. The interesting thing is that world heritage sites
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: ənd ɪf ju 'ɪnˈkroʊʧ ɔn ˈsəmθɪŋ', ɪt minz ju 'muv bɪɔnd əkˈsɛptəbəl ˈlɪmɪts'. ðə ˈɪntəˌrɛstɪŋ θɪŋ ɪz ðət wərld ˈhɛrɪtɪʤ sites

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: only constitute 0.5% of the Earth's surface — so why can't people do their mining and industrial development
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: only* ˈkɑnstəˌtut 0.5* əv ðə ərθs ˈsərfəs —* soʊ waɪ kænt ˈpipəl du ðɛr ˈmaɪnɪŋ ənd ˌɪnˈdəstriəl development

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: on the remaining 99.5%?! But there's one human industry

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: that can actually be beneficial for precious sites — tourism.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: that* kən ˈæˌkʧuəli bi ˌbɛnəˈfɪʃəl fər ˈprɛʃəs saɪts —* tourism.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: But you said earlier that tourism was bad for heritage sites. I know. And I was right, up to a point.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: but* ju sɛd ˈərliər ðət ˈtʊˌrɪzəm wɑz bæd fər ˈhɛrɪtɪʤ saɪts. aɪ noʊ. ənd aɪ wɑz raɪt, əp tɪ ə point.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: World heritage sites are some of tourism's main attractions, and more and more people are visiting them.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: world* ˈhɛrɪtɪʤ saɪts ər səm əv tourism's* meɪn əˈtrækʃənz, ənd mɔr ənd mɔr ˈpipəl ər ˈvɪzɪtɪŋ them.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: So it's all about getting the balance right between generating money to maintain and restore the sites
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: so* ɪts ɔl əˈbaʊt ˈgɪtɪŋ ðə ˈbæləns raɪt bɪtˈwin ˈʤɛnərˌeɪtɪŋ ˈməni tɪ meɪnˈteɪn ənd rɪˈstɔr ðə sites

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: and minimising the impact of tourist activities. — Mm, such as littering and graffiti. — Exactly.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: and* minimising* ðə ˌɪmˈpækt əv ˈtʊrɪst ækˈtɪvɪtiz. —* mm*, səʧ ɛz ˈlɪtərɪŋ ənd grəˈfiti. —* exactly.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: And the term for this is 'sustainable tourism' —
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: and* ðə tərm fər ðɪs ɪz 'səˈsteɪnəbəl ˈtʊˌrɪzəm' —

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: or 'tourism designed to have a low impact on the local culture and the environment, 'while generating employment for local people'.
So UNESCO is working to direct governments, site managers and visitors towards sustainable tourism practices
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: or* 'ˈtʊˌrɪzəm dɪˈzaɪnd tɪ hæv ə loʊ ˌɪmˈpækt ɔn ðə ˈloʊkəl ˈkəlʧər ənd ðə ɪnˈvaɪrənmənt, 'waɪl ˈʤɛnərˌeɪtɪŋ ɪmˈplɔɪmənt fər ˈloʊkəl people'.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: in order to keep our world's natural and cultural heritage safe for future generations.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: so* juˈnɛˌskoʊ ɪz ˈwərkɪŋ tɪ dɪˈrɛkt ˈgəvərnmənts, saɪt ˈmænɪʤərz ənd ˈvɪzɪtərz təˈwɔrdz səˈsteɪnəbəl ˈtʊˌrɪzəm practices

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Wow, you can really talk the talk, Alice. You should work for UNESCO!
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: in* ˈɔrdər tɪ kip ɑr wərldz ˈnæʧərəl ənd ˈkəlʧərəl ˈhɛrɪtɪʤ seɪf fər fˈjuʧər generations.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: OK, let's move on now, and listen to Paul Crocombe, of the Snorkelling and Diving Company in Townsville, Queensland, Australia,
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: wow*, ju kən ˈrɪli tɔk ðə tɔk, ˈælɪs. ju ʃʊd wərk fər unesco!

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: giving his view on how the Great Barrier Reef will cope with threats to its survival.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: ok*, lɛts muv ɔn naʊ, ənd ˈlɪsən tɪ pɔl crocombe*, əv ðə snorkelling* ənd ˈdaɪvɪŋ ˈkəmpəˌni ɪn townsville*, kˈwinzˌlænd, australia,

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: The Reef's fairly dynamic, it's been through a couple of Ice Ages, and is still here, so its resilience will ensure
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: ðə reef's* ˈfɛrli daɪˈnæmɪk, ɪts bɪn θru ə ˈkəpəl əv aɪs ˈeɪʤɪz, ənd ɪz stɪl hir, soʊ ɪts rɪˈzɪljəns wɪl ensure

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: that the Reef is still here in years to come. But the species diversity and the, the visual aesthetics of the Reef
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: that* ðə rif ɪz stɪl hir ɪn jɪrz tɪ kəm. bət ðə ˈspiʃiz dɪˈvərsɪti ənd ðə, ðə ˈvɪʒəwəl ɛsˈθɛtɪks əv ðə reef

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: may change quite considerably, especially if we get an increase in sea temperature,
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: may* ʧeɪnʤ kwaɪt kənˈsɪdərəbli, əˈspɛʃəli ɪf wi gɪt ən ˌɪnˈkris ɪn si temperature,

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: an increase in carbon dioxide in the water, and things like that. Paul Crocombe describes the Reef as 'dynamic' and 'resilient'.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: an* ˌɪnˈkris ɪn ˈkɑrbən daɪˈɑkˌsaɪd ɪn ðə ˈwɔtər, ənd θɪŋz laɪk ðət. pɔl crocombe* dɪˈskraɪbz ðə rif ɛz 'daɪˈnæmɪk' ənd 'resilient'.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: 'Dynamic' means 'active or capable of changing' and 'resilience' means 'the ability to recover or adapt to change' —
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: 'dynamic*' minz 'ˈæktɪv ər ˈkeɪpəbəl əv ˈʧeɪnʤɪŋ' ənd 'rɪˈzɪljəns' minz 'ðə əˈbɪləˌti tɪ rɪˈkəvər ər əˈdæpt tɪ ʧeɪnʤ' —

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: which all sounds good. But Paul also says that the 'species diversity' — 'the range of plants and animals' — may change.
For example, a rise in sea temperature would cause a rise in carbon dioxide levels —
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: which* ɔl saʊnz gʊd. bət pɔl ˈɔlsoʊ sɪz ðət ðə 'ˈspiʃiz dɪˈvərsɪti' —* 'ðə reɪnʤ əv plænts ənd ˈænəməlz' —* meɪ change.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: and this could have a big impact on both the species diversity
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: for* ɪgˈzæmpəl, ə raɪz ɪn si ˈtɛmpərəʧər wʊd kɔz ə raɪz ɪn ˈkɑrbən daɪˈɑkˌsaɪd ˈlɛvəlz —

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: and the Reef's 'visual aesthetic' — or 'the way it looks'.
OK, I think it's time now for the answer to today's quiz question, Alice. Yes, I think so too.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: and* ðɪs kʊd hæv ə bɪg ˌɪmˈpækt ɔn boʊθ ðə ˈspiʃiz diversity

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: OK, so, I asked you which sea creature poses a potential threat to the Great Barrier Reef's ecosystem?
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: ok*, soʊ, aɪ æst ju wɪʧ si ˈkriʧər ˈpoʊzɪz ə pəˈtɛnʃəl θrɛt tɪ ðə greɪt ˈbɛriər reef's* ecosystem?

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Is it a type of a) Starfish? b) Jellyfish? Or c) Cuttlefish?
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: well*, wɪr ˈtɔkɪŋ əˈbaʊt wərld ˈhɛrɪtɪʤ saɪts təˈdeɪ, wɪʧ ər ˈpleɪsɪz juˈnɛˌskoʊ kənˈsɪdərz tɪ bi æt risk

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: And I said b) Jellyfish. Mm, and you got stung, I'm afraid, Neil! — Ow! — Mm.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: and* ðə reef's* 'ˈvɪʒəwəl ɛsˈθɛtɪk' —* ər 'ðə weɪ ɪt looks'.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: The answer is starfish. The crown-of-thorns starfish preys on coral
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: ok*, aɪ θɪŋk ɪts taɪm naʊ fər ðə ˈænsər tɪ ˈtudeɪz kwɪz kˈwɛʃən, ˈælɪs. jɛs, aɪ θɪŋk soʊ too.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: and large outbreaks of these starfish can devastate reefs. Now, can you remind us of some of today's vocabulary, Neil? Sure.
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: and* aɪ sɛd bi) ˈʤɛliˌfɪʃ. mm*, ənd ju gɑt stəŋ, əm əˈfreɪd, nil! —* oʊ! —* mm.

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: Heritage, encroach on something, sustainable tourism, dynamic, resilience,
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: the* ˈænsər ɪz ˈstɑrˌfɪʃ. ðə crown-of-thorns* ˈstɑrˌfɪʃ preɪz ɔn coral

OA1iXnZ0lNw.text: species diversity, visual aesthetic. Well, that's the end of today's 6 Minute English. Don't forget to join us again soon!
OA1iXnZ0lNw.ipa: species* dɪˈvərsɪti, ˈvɪʒəwəl ɛsˈθɛtɪk. wɛl, ðæts ðə ɛnd əv ˈtudeɪz 6 ˈmɪnət ˈɪŋlɪʃ. doʊnt fərˈgɛt tɪ ʤɔɪn ˈjuˈɛs əˈgɛn soon!
