lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The human immune system is the most complex biological system we know, after the human brain,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə hjˈumən ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm ˈɪz ðə mˈoʊst kˈɑmplɛks bˌaɪəlˈɑdʒɪkəl sˈɪstəm wˈi nˈoʊ ˈæftɚ ðə hjˈumən bɹˈeɪn/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and yet most of us never learn how it works.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd jˈɛt mˈoʊst ˈʌv ˈʌs nˈɛvɚ lˈɝn hˈaʊ ˈɪt wˈɝks/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Or, what it is.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɔɹ wˈʌt ˈɪt ˈɪz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Your immune system consists of hundreds of tiny—and two large—organs.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm kənsˈɪsts ˈʌv hˈʌndɹədz ˈʌv tinyand tˈu largeorgans/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: It has its own transport network spread throughout your body.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɪt hˈæz ˈɪts ˈoʊn tɹænspˈɔɹt nˈɛtwˌɝk spɹˈɛd θɹuˈaʊt jˈɔɹ bˈɑdi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Every day, it makes hundreds of billions of fresh cells organized like an army.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɛvɚi dˈeɪ ˈɪt mˈeɪks hˈʌndɹədz ˈʌv bˈɪljənz ˈʌv fɹˈɛʃ sˈɛlz ˈɔɹɡənˌaɪzd lˈaɪk ˈæn ˈɑɹmi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: With soldiers, captains, intelligence officers, heavy weapons, and crazy suicide bombers.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈɪð sˈoʊldʒɚz kˈæptənz ˌɪntˈɛlədʒəns ˈɔfəsɚz hˈɛvi wˈɛpənz ənd kɹˈeɪzi sˈuəsˌaɪd bˈɑmɚz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: It's not some sort of abstract entity.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɪts nˈɑt sˈʌm sˈɔɹt ˈʌv æbstɹˈækt ˈɛntəti/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Your immune system is you.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm ˈɪz jˈu/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Your biology protecting you from the billions of microorganisms that want to consume you,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ baɪˈɑlədʒi pɹətˈɛktɪŋ jˈu fɹˈʌm ðə bˈɪljənz ˈʌv mˌaɪkɹoʊˈɔɹɡənˌɪzəmz ðˈæt wˈɑnt tˈu kənsˈum jˈu/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and from your own perverted cells that turn into cancer.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd fɹˈʌm jˈɔɹ ˈoʊn pɚvˈɝtɪd sˈɛlz ðˈæt tˈɝn ˈɪntu kˈænsɚ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: It's so manifold that it's impossible to cover in one video,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɪts sˈoʊ mˈænəfˌoʊld ðˈæt ˈɪts ˌɪmpˈɑsəbəl tˈu kˈʌvɚ ɪn wˈʌn vˈɪdioʊ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: so we'll make a series looking at different aspects of it.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /sˈoʊ wˈil mˈeɪk ə sˈɪɹiz lˈʊkɪŋ ˈæt dˈɪfɚənt ˈæspˌɛkts ˈʌv ˈɪt/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Today: What happens when your body is invaded
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /tədˈeɪ wˈʌt hˈæpənz wˈɛn jˈɔɹ bˈɑdi ˈɪz ˌɪnvˈeɪdəd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and your first lines of defenses are engaged in a fight for life and death.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd jˈɔɹ fˈɝst lˈaɪnz ˈʌv dɪfˈɛnsəz ˈɑɹ ɛnɡˈeɪdʒd ɪn ə fˈaɪt fˈɔɹ lˈaɪf ənd dˈɛθ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: It's been a normal day,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɪts bˈɪn ə nˈɔɹməl dˈeɪ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: when suddenly the world explodes and an asteroid rips the sky open.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈɛn sˈʌdənli ðə wˈɝld ɪksplˈoʊdz ənd ˈæn ˈæstɚˌɔɪd ɹˈɪps ðə skˈaɪ ˈoʊpən/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Countless alien life forms invade,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /kˈaʊntləs ˈeɪliən lˈaɪf fˈɔɹmz ˌɪnvˈeɪd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: ready to destroy cities and infrastructure, and eat civilians...
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ɹˈɛdi tˈu dɪstɹˈɔɪ sˈɪtiz ənd ˌɪnfɹəstɹˈʌktʃɚ ənd ˈit səvˈɪljənz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Or, this is what your cells experience.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɔɹ ðˈɪs ˈɪz wˈʌt jˈɔɹ sˈɛlz ɪkspˈɪɹiəns/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: You look at your bleeding thumb that you just cut on a dirty twig in the park.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈu lˈʊk ˈæt jˈɔɹ blˈidɪŋ θˈʌm ðˈæt jˈu dʒˈʌst kˈʌt ˈɑn ə dˈɝti twˈɪɡ ɪn ðə pˈɑɹk/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: How annoying!
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /hˈaʊ ənˈɔɪɪŋ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But inside the wound, a horrible catastrophe has happened.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt ˌɪnsˈaɪd ðə wˈaʊnd ə hˈɔɹəbəl kətˈæstɹəfi hˈæz hˈæpənd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: There are dead cells, and blood and dirt everywhere.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈɛɹ ˈɑɹ dˈɛd sˈɛlz ənd blˈʌd ənd dˈɝt ˈɛvɹiwˌɛɹ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Even worse, countless bacteria invade the warm caverns between your helpless cells
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈivɪn wˈɝs kˈaʊntləs bæktˈɪɹiə ˌɪnvˈeɪd ðə wˈɔɹm kˈævɚnz bɪtwˈin jˈɔɹ hˈɛlpləs sˈɛlz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: to explore their new home, steal your resources, and poop everywhere.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /tˈu ɪksplˈɔɹ ðˈɛɹ nˈu hˈoʊm stˈil jˈɔɹ ɹˈisɔɹsɪz ənd pˈup ˈɛvɹiwˌɛɹ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Immediately, the first stage of your defense kicks in.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˌɪmˈidˌiətli ðə fˈɝst stˈeɪdʒ ˈʌv jˈɔɹ dɪfˈɛns kˈɪks ɪn/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The cells that survive the impact, or are hurt or dying, scream in panic
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə sˈɛlz ðˈæt sɚvˈaɪv ðə ˌɪmpˈækt ˈɔɹ ˈɑɹ hˈɝt ˈɔɹ dˈaɪɪŋ skɹˈim ɪn pˈænɪk/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: releasing an onslaught of chemical alarm signals that awaken your immune system.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ɹilˈisɪŋ ˈæn ˈɔnslˌɔt ˈʌv kˈɛməkəl əlˈɑɹm sˈɪɡnəlz ðˈæt əwˈeɪkən jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The first cells to show up are macrophages.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə fˈɝst sˈɛlz tˈu ʃˈoʊ ˈʌp ˈɑɹ mˈækɹoʊfˌeɪdʒɪz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: If an average cell were the size of a human, a macrophage would be the size of a black rhino.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɪf ˈæn ˈævɚɪdʒ sˈɛl wˈɝ ðə sˈaɪz ˈʌv ə hjˈumən ə mˈækɹoʊfˌeɪdʒ wˈʊd bˈi ðə sˈaɪz ˈʌv ə blˈæk ɹˈaɪnˌoʊ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: A stoic cell in principle but you wouldn't want to annoy it.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ə stˈoʊɪk sˈɛl ɪn pɹˈɪnsəpəl bˈʌt jˈu wˈʊdənt wˈɑnt tˈu ənˈɔɪ ˈɪt/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Bacteria -do- annoy them.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bæktˈɪɹiə dˈu ənˈɔɪ ðˈɛm/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Within seconds, the large cells attack and begin killing them without mercy.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wɪðˈɪn sˈɛkəndz ðə lˈɑɹdʒ sˈɛlz ətˈæk ənd bɪɡˈɪn kˈɪlɪŋ ðˈɛm wɪθˈaʊt mˈɝsi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: They stretch out parts like the arms of an octopus,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈeɪ stɹˈɛtʃ ˈaʊt pˈɑɹts lˈaɪk ðə ˈɑɹmz ˈʌv ˈæn ˈɑktəpˌʊs/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and grab the bacteria to swallow them whole and digest them alive.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd ɡɹˈæb ðə bæktˈɪɹiə tˈu swˈɑloʊ ðˈɛm hˈoʊl ənd daɪdʒˈɛst ðˈɛm əlˈaɪv/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: A macrophage can eat 100 bacteria before it's exhausted.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ə mˈækɹoʊfˌeɪdʒ kˈæn ˈit 100 bæktˈɪɹiə bɪfˈɔɹ ˈɪts ɪɡzˈɔstɪd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But there are too many enemies, so the macrophages call for reinforcements.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt ðˈɛɹ ˈɑɹ tˈu mˈɛni ˈɛnəmiz sˈoʊ ðə mˈækɹoʊfˌeɪdʒɪz kˈɔl fˈɔɹ ɹˌiɪnfˈɔɹsmənts/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: In your blood, hundreds of thousands of neutrophils pick up their signals and move to the battlefield.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ɪn jˈɔɹ blˈʌd hˈʌndɹədz ˈʌv θˈaʊzəndz ˈʌv neutrophils pˈɪk ˈʌp ðˈɛɹ sˈɪɡnəlz ənd mˈuv tˈu ðə bˈætəlfˌild/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Neutrophils are intense suicide warriors that only live to kill.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /neutrophils ˈɑɹ ˌɪntˈɛns sˈuəsˌaɪd wˈɔɹiɚz ðˈæt ˈoʊnli lˈaɪv tˈu kˈɪl/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: They're so enthusiastic about killing that they kill themselves a few days after birth,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈɛɹ sˈoʊ ɪnθˌuziˈæstɪk əbˈaʊt kˈɪlɪŋ ðˈæt ðˈeɪ kˈɪl ðɛmsˈɛlvz ə fjˈu dˈeɪz ˈæftɚ bˈɝθ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: so they don't have time to accidentally destroy your body from the inside.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /sˈoʊ ðˈeɪ dˈoʊnt hˈæv tˈaɪm tˈu ˌæksədˈɛntəli dɪstɹˈɔɪ jˈɔɹ bˈɑdi fɹˈʌm ðə ˌɪnsˈaɪd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: As soon as neutrophils arrive, they begin vomiting deadly chemicals at bacteria, or devour them.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈæz sˈun ˈæz neutrophils ɚˈaɪv ðˈeɪ bɪɡˈɪn vˈɑmətɪŋ dˈɛdli kˈɛmɪkəlz ˈæt bæktˈɪɹiə ˈɔɹ dɪvˈaʊɚ ðˈɛm/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: They are so careless in their attacks that they are causing real damage to your own cells.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈeɪ ˈɑɹ sˈoʊ kˈɛɹləs ɪn ðˈɛɹ ətˈæks ðˈæt ðˈeɪ ˈɑɹ kˈɑzɪŋ ɹˈil dˈæmədʒ tˈu jˈɔɹ ˈoʊn sˈɛlz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But collateral damage is not their concern now,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt kəlˈætɚəl dˈæmədʒ ˈɪz nˈɑt ðˈɛɹ kənsˈɝn nˈaʊ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: or ever.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɔɹ ˈɛvɚ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Some neutrophils go so far to push their suicide button and explode,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /sˈʌm neutrophils ɡˈoʊ sˈoʊ fˈɑɹ tˈu pˈʊʃ ðˈɛɹ sˈuəsˌaɪd bˈʌtən ənd ɪksplˈoʊd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: casting a wide and toxic net made from their own DNA filled with deadly chemicals that trap and kill bacteria.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /kˈæstɪŋ ə wˈaɪd ənd tˈɑksɪk nˈɛt mˈeɪd fɹˈʌm ðˈɛɹ ˈoʊn dˈiˌɛnˈeɪ fˈɪld wˈɪð dˈɛdli kˈɛmɪkəlz ðˈæt tɹˈæp ənd kˈɪl bæktˈɪɹiə/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Sometimes, they can continue fighting after that, even though they're sort of dead already.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /səmtˈaɪmz ðˈeɪ kˈæn kəntˈɪnju fˈaɪtɪŋ ˈæftɚ ðˈæt ˈivɪn ðˈoʊ ðˈɛɹ sˈɔɹt ˈʌv dˈɛd ɔlɹˈɛdi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: This is how much fun they have killing!
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈɪs ˈɪz hˈaʊ mˈʌtʃ fˈʌn ðˈeɪ hˈæv kˈɪlɪŋ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: While the battle rages, your blood vessels let fluid stream into the battlefield like a dam opening up towards a valley.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈaɪl ðə bˈætəl ɹˈeɪdʒɪz jˈɔɹ blˈʌd vˈɛsəlz lˈɛt flˈuəd stɹˈim ˈɪntu ðə bˈætəlfˌild lˈaɪk ə dˈæm ˈoʊpənɪŋ ˈʌp təwˈɔɹdz ə vˈæli/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: You notice this as inflammation.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈu nˈoʊtəs ðˈɪs ˈæz ˌɪnfləmˈeɪʃən/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Your thumb swells up a little and gets red and warm.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ θˈʌm swˈɛlz ˈʌp ə lˈɪtəl ənd ɡˈɛts ɹˈɛd ənd wˈɔɹm/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The fluid brings a silent killer into the battle zone, millions of complement proteins.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə flˈuəd bɹˈɪŋz ə sˈaɪlənt kˈɪlɚ ˈɪntu ðə bˈætəl zˈoʊn mˈɪljənz ˈʌv kˈɑmpləmənt pɹˈoʊtˌinz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: A sort of automated liquid weapon that stuns and kills bacteria by ripping holes into them.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ə sˈɔɹt ˈʌv ˈɔtəmˌeɪtɪd lˈɪkwəd wˈɛpən ðˈæt stˈʌnz ənd kˈɪlz bæktˈɪɹiə bˈaɪ ɹˈɪpɪŋ hˈoʊlz ˈɪntu ðˈɛm/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: We made a whole video explaining them in detail.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈi mˈeɪd ə hˈoʊl vˈɪdioʊ ɪksplˈeɪnɪŋ ðˈɛm ɪn dɪtˈeɪl/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: We are reaching a crossroad now.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈi ˈɑɹ ɹˈitʃɪŋ ə kɹˈɔsɹˌoʊd nˈaʊ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: If things go well, your first line of defense kills the invaders quickly.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɪf θˈɪŋz ɡˈoʊ wˈɛl jˈɔɹ fˈɝst lˈaɪn ˈʌv dɪfˈɛns kˈɪlz ðə ˌɪnvˈeɪdɚz kwˈɪkli/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But sometimes, the enemies are too strong and would overwhelm your defenses eventually,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt səmtˈaɪmz ðə ˈɛnəmiz ˈɑɹ tˈu stɹˈɔŋ ənd wˈʊd ˌoʊvɚwˈɛlm jˈɔɹ dɪfˈɛnsəz ɪvˈɛntʃəwəli/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: which means certain death for you, the human.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈɪtʃ mˈinz sˈɝtən dˈɛθ fˈɔɹ jˈu ðə hjˈumən/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: This is the hour of the dendritic cell, your immune system's intelligence officer.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈɪs ˈɪz ðə ˈaʊɚ ˈʌv ðə dɛndɹˈɪtɪk sˈɛl jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəmz ˌɪntˈɛlədʒəns ˈɔfəsɚ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: While your soldiers were bashing in heads,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈaɪl jˈɔɹ sˈoʊldʒɚz wˈɝ bˈæʃɪŋ ɪn hˈɛdz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: it was collecting samples by ripping bacteria into tiny parts and covering itself in it.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɪt wˈɑz kəlˈɛktɪŋ sˈæmpəlz bˈaɪ ɹˈɪpɪŋ bæktˈɪɹiə ˈɪntu tˈaɪni pˈɑɹts ənd kˈʌvɚɪŋ ˌɪtsˈɛlf ɪn ˈɪt/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Like a soldier decorating itself in the guts of a dead enemy.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /lˈaɪk ə sˈoʊldʒɚ dˈɛkɚˌeɪtɪŋ ˌɪtsˈɛlf ɪn ðə ɡˈʌts ˈʌv ə dˈɛd ˈɛnəmi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The cell leaves the battlefield and enters the superhighway of your immune system
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə sˈɛl lˈivz ðə bˈætəlfˌild ənd ˈɛntɚz ðə sˌupɚhˈaɪwˌeɪ ˈʌv jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: that connects all your tissues with your immune headquarters.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈæt kənˈɛkts ˈɔl jˈɔɹ tˈɪsjˌuz wˈɪð jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun hˈɛdkwˌɔɹtɚz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Your lymph nodes.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ lˈɪmf nˈoʊdz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The dendritic cell coming from the battlefield is looking for a helper T cell,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə dɛndɹˈɪtɪk sˈɛl kˈʌmɪŋ fɹˈʌm ðə bˈætəlfˌild ˈɪz lˈʊkɪŋ fˈɔɹ ə hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛl/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: which is a sort of all-purpose commander cell within your immune army.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈɪtʃ ˈɪz ə sˈɔɹt ˈʌv allpurpose kəmˈændɚ sˈɛl wɪðˈɪn jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun ˈɑɹmi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But not any helper T cell.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt nˈɑt ˈɛni hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛl/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: One that happens to have just the right weapon for the bacteria that infected your wound.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈʌn ðˈæt hˈæpənz tˈu hˈæv dʒˈʌst ðə ɹˈaɪt wˈɛpən fˈɔɹ ðə bæktˈɪɹiə ðˈæt ˌɪnfˈɛktɪd jˈɔɹ wˈaʊnd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: So it goes around and rubs itself, still covered in bacteria parts, against every helper T cell it meets.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /sˈoʊ ˈɪt ɡˈoʊz ɚˈaʊnd ənd ɹˈʌbz ˌɪtsˈɛlf stˈɪl kˈʌvɚd ɪn bæktˈɪɹiə pˈɑɹts əɡˈɛnst ˈɛvɚi hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛl ˈɪt mˈits/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Most T cells are a bit disgusted and not interested.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /mˈoʊst tˈi sˈɛlz ˈɑɹ ə bˈɪt dɪsɡˈʌstəd ənd nˈɑt ˈɪntɹəstɪd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But after a few hours, something clicks.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt ˈæftɚ ə fjˈu ˈaʊɚz sˈʌmθɪŋ klˈɪks/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: A helper T cell recognizes the bacteria parts.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ə hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛl ɹˈɛkəɡnˌaɪzəz ðə bæktˈɪɹiə pˈɑɹts/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: This cell is the weapon that's needed right now.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈɪs sˈɛl ˈɪz ðə wˈɛpən ðˈæts nˈidəd ɹˈaɪt nˈaʊ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The dendritic cell is overjoyed and activates the helper T cell.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə dɛndɹˈɪtɪk sˈɛl ˈɪz ˌoʊvɚdʒˈɔɪd ənd ˈæktəvˌeɪts ðə hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛl/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Okay, wait.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˌoʊkˈeɪ wˈeɪt/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: How come your immune system has a cell that has a weapon against the specific bacteria that infected you?
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /hˈaʊ kˈʌm jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm hˈæz ə sˈɛl ðˈæt hˈæz ə wˈɛpən əɡˈɛnst ðə spəsˈɪfɪk bæktˈɪɹiə ðˈæt ˌɪnfˈɛktɪd jˈu/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Well, your immune system has a perfect weapon against every possible disease in the universe.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈɛl jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm hˈæz ə pɚfˈɛkt wˈɛpən əɡˈɛnst ˈɛvɚi pˈɑsəbəl dɪzˈiz ɪn ðə jˈunəvˌɝs/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Against the Black Death, the Coronavirus, or an infection that will emerge in 100 years on Mars.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /əɡˈɛnst ðə blˈæk dˈɛθ ðə coronavirus ˈɔɹ ˈæn ˌɪnfˈɛkʃən ðˈæt wˈɪl ɪmˈɝdʒ ɪn 100 jˈɪɹz ˈɑn mˈɑɹz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: We'll talk about this a bit more in the next video because it's very complex.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈil tˈɔk əbˈaʊt ðˈɪs ə bˈɪt mˈɔɹ ɪn ðə nˈɛkst vˈɪdioʊ bɪkˈɔz ˈɪts vˈɛɹi kˈɑmplɛks/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: So, for now, just know that you have billions of unique helper T cells
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /sˈoʊ fˈɔɹ nˈaʊ dʒˈʌst nˈoʊ ðˈæt jˈu hˈæv bˈɪljənz ˈʌv junˈik hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛlz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: that each have weapons against every possible enemy.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈæt ˈitʃ hˈæv wˈɛpənz əɡˈɛnst ˈɛvɚi pˈɑsəbəl ˈɛnəmi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: After the right T cell is activated, your second line of defense awakes
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈæftɚ ðə ɹˈaɪt tˈi sˈɛl ˈɪz ˈæktəvˌeɪtɪd jˈɔɹ sˈɛkənd lˈaɪn ˈʌv dɪfˈɛns awakes/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and rises like a teenager that needs to get up on a school day...
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd ɹˈaɪzəz lˈaɪk ə tˈinˌeɪdʒɚ ðˈæt nˈidz tˈu ɡˈɛt ˈʌp ˈɑn ə skˈul dˈeɪ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Very slowly.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /vˈɛɹi slˈoʊli/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Your heavy weapons are incredibly effective but they're not fast.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ hˈɛvi wˈɛpənz ˈɑɹ ˌɪnkɹˈɛdəbli ɪfˈɛktɪv bˈʌt ðˈɛɹ nˈɑt fˈæst/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The activated helper T cell begins to clone itself over and over again.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə ˈæktəvˌeɪtɪd hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛl bɪɡˈɪnz tˈu klˈoʊn ˌɪtsˈɛlf ˈoʊvɚ ənd ˈoʊvɚ əɡˈɛn/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: One becomes two, two become four, until there are thousands of them.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈʌn bɪkˈʌmz tˈu tˈu bɪkˈʌm fˈɔɹ əntˈɪl ðˈɛɹ ˈɑɹ θˈaʊzəndz ˈʌv ðˈɛm/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Now, they split into two groups.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /nˈaʊ ðˈeɪ splˈɪt ˈɪntu tˈu ɡɹˈups/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The first group quickly moves to help out your soldiers.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə fˈɝst ɡɹˈup kwˈɪkli mˈuvz tˈu hˈɛlp ˈaʊt jˈɔɹ sˈoʊldʒɚz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: At the battlefield, things are getting out of hand.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈæt ðə bˈætəlfˌild θˈɪŋz ˈɑɹ ɡˈɛtɪŋ ˈaʊt ˈʌv hˈænd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: A tired macrophage is ready to give up.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ə tˈaɪɚd mˈækɹoʊfˌeɪdʒ ˈɪz ɹˈɛdi tˈu ɡˈɪv ˈʌp/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: After fighting for days, it just wants to go to sleep like many of its buddies have done already.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈæftɚ fˈaɪtɪŋ fˈɔɹ dˈeɪz ˈɪt dʒˈʌst wˈɑnts tˈu ɡˈoʊ tˈu slˈip lˈaɪk mˈɛni ˈʌv ˈɪts bˈʌdiz hˈæv dˈʌn ɔlɹˈɛdi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But now the helper T cells arrive.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt nˈaʊ ðə hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛlz ɚˈaɪv/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: One of them comes to the tired macrophage and whispers something using special chemical signals.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈʌn ˈʌv ðˈɛm kˈʌmz tˈu ðə tˈaɪɚd mˈækɹoʊfˌeɪdʒ ənd wˈɪspɚz sˈʌmθɪŋ jˈuzɪŋ spˈɛʃəl kˈɛməkəl sˈɪɡnəlz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: In a heartbeat, the demoralized soldier feels fresh again.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ɪn ə hˈɑɹtbˌit ðə dɪmˈɔɹəlˌaɪzd sˈoʊldʒɚ fˈilz fɹˈɛʃ əɡˈɛn/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But there's something else.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt ðˈɛɹz sˈʌmθɪŋ ˈɛls/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: A hot white anger.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ə hˈɑt wˈaɪt ˈæŋɡɚ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The macrophage knows what it needs to do...
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə mˈækɹoʊfˌeɪdʒ nˈoʊz wˈʌt ˈɪt nˈidz tˈu dˈu/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Kill.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /kˈɪl/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Invigorated, it throws itself against the enemies once again.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˌɪnvˈɪɡɚˌeɪtɪd ˈɪt θɹˈoʊz ˌɪtsˈɛlf əɡˈɛnst ðə ˈɛnəmiz wˈʌns əɡˈɛn/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: All over the battlefield, this begins to happen.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɔl ˈoʊvɚ ðə bˈætəlfˌild ðˈɪs bɪɡˈɪnz tˈu hˈæpən/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Meanwhile, the second group of helper T cells was working on activating another line of defense:
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /mˈinwˌaɪl ðə sˈɛkənd ɡɹˈup ˈʌv hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛlz wˈɑz wˈɝkɪŋ ˈɑn ˈæktəvˌeɪtɪŋ ənˈʌðɚ lˈaɪn ˈʌv dɪfˈɛns/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: B cells, your antibody factories.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈi sˈɛlz jˈɔɹ ˈæntɪbˌɑdi fˈæktɚiz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Antibodies are protein super weapons that look like tiny crabs with two pincers to grab enemies.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈæntɪbˌɑdiz ˈɑɹ pɹˈoʊtˌin sˈupɚ wˈɛpənz ðˈæt lˈʊk lˈaɪk tˈaɪni kɹˈæbz wˈɪð tˈu pˈɪnsɚz tˈu ɡɹˈæb ˈɛnəmiz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Just like the helper T cells, there are B cells in your body
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /dʒˈʌst lˈaɪk ðə hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛlz ðˈɛɹ ˈɑɹ bˈi sˈɛlz ɪn jˈɔɹ bˈɑdi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: that are able to make just the right antibodies for every possible enemy.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈæt ˈɑɹ ˈeɪbəl tˈu mˈeɪk dʒˈʌst ðə ɹˈaɪt ˈæntɪbˌɑdiz fˈɔɹ ˈɛvɚi pˈɑsəbəl ˈɛnəmi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: And the helper T cell is looking for exactly these B cells.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd ðə hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛl ˈɪz lˈʊkɪŋ fˈɔɹ ɪɡzˈæktli ðˈiz bˈi sˈɛlz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: After a day or two, the right B cell is found, and begins to clone itself.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈæftɚ ə dˈeɪ ˈɔɹ tˈu ðə ɹˈaɪt bˈi sˈɛl ˈɪz fˈaʊnd ənd bɪɡˈɪnz tˈu klˈoʊn ˌɪtsˈɛlf/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: As soon as enough clones have been made,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈæz sˈun ˈæz ɪnˈʌf klˈoʊnz hˈæv bˈɪn mˈeɪd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: each B cell begins pumping out up to 2,000 antibodies per second!
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈitʃ bˈi sˈɛl bɪɡˈɪnz pˈʌmpɪŋ ˈaʊt ˈʌp tˈu 2000 ˈæntɪbˌɑdiz pˈɝ sˈɛkənd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: About a week after you injured yourself and bacteria invaded,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /əbˈaʊt ə wˈik ˈæftɚ jˈu ˈɪndʒɚd jɚsˈɛlf ənd bæktˈɪɹiə ˌɪnvˈeɪdəd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: your second line of defense finally arrives in full force.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ sˈɛkənd lˈaɪn ˈʌv dɪfˈɛns fˈaɪnəli ɚˈaɪvz ɪn fˈʊl fˈɔɹs/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The tiny army begins to saturate the battlefield, pinching and stunning desperate bacteria.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə tˈaɪni ˈɑɹmi bɪɡˈɪnz tˈu sˈætʃɚˌeɪt ðə bˈætəlfˌild pˈɪntʃɪŋ ənd stˈʌnɪŋ dˈɛspɹɪt bæktˈɪɹiə/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The antibodies clump them together and make them unable to move or fight,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə ˈæntɪbˌɑdiz klˈʌmp ðˈɛm təɡˈɛðɚ ənd mˈeɪk ðˈɛm ənˈeɪbəl tˈu mˈuv ˈɔɹ fˈaɪt/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: while your soldiers massacre the defenseless victims.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈaɪl jˈɔɹ sˈoʊldʒɚz mˈæsəkɚ ðə dɪfˈɛnsləs vˈɪktəmz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The tide is turning fast.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə tˈaɪd ˈɪz tˈɝnɪŋ fˈæst/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: As the last enemies are cleaned up, your soldiers realize they are no longer needed,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈæz ðə lˈæst ˈɛnəmiz ˈɑɹ klˈind ˈʌp jˈɔɹ sˈoʊldʒɚz ɹˈiəlˌaɪz ðˈeɪ ˈɑɹ nˈoʊ lˈɔŋɡɚ nˈidəd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and begin to kill themselves to save resources.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd bɪɡˈɪn tˈu kˈɪl ðɛmsˈɛlvz tˈu sˈeɪv ɹˈisɔɹsɪz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But not all of them.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt nˈɑt ˈɔl ˈʌv ðˈɛm/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: A few helper T cells remain and turn into memory cells.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ə fjˈu hˈɛlpɚ tˈi sˈɛlz ɹɪmˈeɪn ənd tˈɝn ˈɪntu mˈɛmɚi sˈɛlz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: They will guard the tissue for years,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈeɪ wˈɪl ɡˈɑɹd ðə tˈɪsjˌu fˈɔɹ jˈɪɹz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: making sure the same bacteria will never again gain a foothold here.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /mˈeɪkɪŋ ʃˈʊɹ ðə sˈeɪm bæktˈɪɹiə wˈɪl nˈɛvɚ əɡˈɛn ɡˈeɪn ə fˈʊthˌoʊld hˈiɹ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Similarly, a few B cells will stay alive
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /sˈɪməlɚli ə fjˈu bˈi sˈɛlz wˈɪl stˈeɪ əlˈaɪv/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and keep producing a low amount of antibodies,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd kˈip pɹədˈusɪŋ ə lˈoʊ əmˈaʊnt ˈʌv ˈæntɪbˌɑdiz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: making you immune against this bacteria,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /mˈeɪkɪŋ jˈu ˌɪmjˈun əɡˈɛnst ðˈɪs bæktˈɪɹiə/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: maybe for the rest of your life.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /mˈeɪbi fˈɔɹ ðə ɹˈɛst ˈʌv jˈɔɹ lˈaɪf/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: One day you wake up and notice that the wound has grown over
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈʌn dˈeɪ jˈu wˈeɪk ˈʌp ənd nˈoʊtəs ðˈæt ðə wˈaʊnd hˈæz ɡɹˈoʊn ˈoʊvɚ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and left nothing but a faint red mark.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd lˈɛft nˈʌθɪŋ bˈʌt ə fˈeɪnt ɹˈɛd mˈɑɹk/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: You were completely unaware of the drama your cells had to deal with.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /jˈu wˈɝ kəmplˈitli ˌʌnəwˈɛɹ ˈʌv ðə dɹˈɑmə jˈɔɹ sˈɛlz hˈæd tˈu dˈil wˈɪð/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: For you, the whole ordeal was a slight annoyance.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /fˈɔɹ jˈu ðə hˈoʊl ɔɹdˈil wˈɑz ə slˈaɪt ənˈɔɪəns/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: While for millions of cells, it was a desperate fight for life and death.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /wˈaɪl fˈɔɹ mˈɪljənz ˈʌv sˈɛlz ˈɪt wˈɑz ə dˈɛspɹɪt fˈaɪt fˈɔɹ lˈaɪf ənd dˈɛθ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: But this is just the beginning of the epic story that unfolds inside you every day,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bˈʌt ðˈɪs ˈɪz dʒˈʌst ðə bɪɡˈɪnɪŋ ˈʌv ðə ˈɛpɪk stˈɔɹi ðˈæt ənfˈoʊldz ˌɪnsˈaɪd jˈu ˈɛvɚi dˈeɪ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and is told in full in
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd ˈɪz tˈoʊld ɪn fˈʊl ɪn/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: "Immune: A Journey into the Mysterious System That Keeps You Alive",
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˌɪmjˈun ə dʒˈɝni ˈɪntu ðə mɪstˈɪɹiəs sˈɪstəm ðˈæt kˈips jˈu əlˈaɪv/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: written by Philipp Dettmer, the founder and head writer of Kurzgesagt.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ɹˈɪtən bˈaɪ fˈɪlɪp dˈɛtmɚ ðə fˈaʊndɚ ənd hˈɛd ɹˈaɪtɚ ˈʌv kurzgesagt/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The book is a beautifully illustrated deep dive into the immune system,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə bˈʊk ˈɪz ə bjˈutəfli ˈɪləstɹˌeɪtəd dˈip dˈaɪv ˈɪntu ðə ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: the most important thing you don't know enough about,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə mˈoʊst ˌɪmpˈɔɹtənt θˈɪŋ jˈu dˈoʊnt nˈoʊ ɪnˈʌf əbˈaʊt/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: that will forever change how you think about your body.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈæt wˈɪl fɚˈɛvɚ tʃˈeɪndʒ hˈaʊ jˈu θˈɪŋk əbˈaʊt jˈɔɹ bˈɑdi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Written to be as simple and fun as possible,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ɹˈɪtən tˈu bˈi ˈæz sˈɪmpəl ənd fˈʌn ˈæz pˈɑsəbəl/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: it's been an ongoing passion project for almost a decade
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ˈɪts bˈɪn ˈæn ˈɑnɡˌoʊɪŋ pˈæʃən pɹˈɑdʒɛkt fˈɔɹ ˈɔlmˌoʊst ə dɛkˈeɪd/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: because the immune system is just about the most amazing and fascinating topic there is.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /bɪkˈɔz ðə ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm ˈɪz dʒˈʌst əbˈaʊt ðə mˈoʊst əmˈeɪzɪŋ ənd fˈæsənˌeɪtɪŋ tˈɑpɪk ðˈɛɹ ˈɪz/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: So, go on a journey and get to know your own body.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /sˈoʊ ɡˈoʊ ˈɑn ə dʒˈɝni ənd ɡˈɛt tˈu nˈoʊ jˈɔɹ ˈoʊn bˈɑdi/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: From mystery organs, murder universities, to the largest library in the universe.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /fɹˈʌm mˈɪstɚi ˈɔɹɡənz mˈɝdɚ jˌunəvˈɝsətiz tˈu ðə lˈɑɹdʒəst lˈaɪbɹɛɹˌi ɪn ðə jˈunəvˌɝs/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Explore how incredibly vast your immune system is and how it actually works.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ɪksplˈɔɹ hˈaʊ ˌɪnkɹˈɛdəbli vˈæst jˈɔɹ ˌɪmjˈun sˈɪstəm ˈɪz ənd hˈaʊ ˈɪt ˈæktʃˌuəli wˈɝks/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: How it fights enemies from cancer to HIV, or just the flu.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /hˈaʊ ˈɪt fˈaɪts ˈɛnəmiz fɹˈʌm kˈænsɚ tˈu ˈeɪtʃˈaɪvˈi#abbrev ˈɔɹ dʒˈʌst ðə flˈu/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Learn how you can boost it and if that's actually a good idea.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /lˈɝn hˈaʊ jˈu kˈæn bˈust ˈɪt ənd ˈɪf ðˈæts ˈæktʃˌuəli ə ɡˈʊd aɪdˈiə/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: The book will be out in six weeks
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðə bˈʊk wˈɪl bˈi ˈaʊt ɪn sˈɪks wˈiks/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and it would mean the world to us and Philipp if you could pre-order it.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd ˈɪt wˈʊd mˈin ðə wˈɝld tˈu ˈʌs ənd fˈɪlɪp ˈɪf jˈu kˈʊd preorder ˈɪt/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Pre-ordering is super important in the world of books,
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /preordering ˈɪz sˈupɚ ˌɪmpˈɔɹtənt ɪn ðə wˈɝld ˈʌv bˈʊks/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and in contrast to games, we can guarantee that it's actually finished
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd ɪn kˈɑntɹæst tˈu ɡˈeɪmz wˈi kˈæn ɡˌɛɹəntˈi ðˈæt ˈɪts ˈæktʃˌuəli fˈɪnɪʃt/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: and there will be no day one patch.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ənd ðˈɛɹ wˈɪl bˈi nˈoʊ dˈeɪ wˈʌn pˈætʃ/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: There's a link in the description.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈɛɹz ə lˈɪŋk ɪn ðə dɪskɹˈɪpʃən/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: This is the end of a decade-long personal journey and we'd love to see what you think.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /ðˈɪs ˈɪz ðə ˈɛnd ˈʌv ə decadelong pˈɝsɪnəl dʒˈɝni ənd wˈid lˈʌv tˈu sˈi wˈʌt jˈu θˈɪŋk/

lXfEK8G8CUI.text: Thank you for watching.
lXfEK8G8CUI.ipa: /θˈæŋk jˈu fˈɔɹ wˈɑtʃɪŋ/

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Alcohol is the most harmful substance on Earth. Every year it kills more people than terrorism,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: alcohol* ɪz ðə moʊst ˈhɑrmfəl ˈsəbstəns ɔn ərθ. ˈɛvəri jɪr ɪt kɪlz mɔr ˈpipəl ðən terrorism,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: wars, homicides and car accidents combined. It injures tens of millions in accidents,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: wars*, ˈhɑməˌsaɪdz ənd kɑr ˈæksədənts kəmˈbaɪnd. ɪt ˈɪnʤərz tɛnz əv ˈmɪljənz ɪn accidents,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: violence and crime; while keeping hundreds of millions trapped in
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: violence* ənd kraɪm; waɪl ˈkipɪŋ ˈhənərdz əv ˈmɪljənz træpt in

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: disease. If it didn’t exist already, inventing it today would be unthinkable.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: disease*. ɪf ɪt didn’t* ɪgˈzɪst ɔˈrɛdi, ˌɪnˈvɛntɪŋ ɪt təˈdeɪ wʊd bi unthinkable.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: And yet, more than 2 billion people drink. Most of us can't imagine a barbecue without beer,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: and* jɛt, mɔr ðən 2 ˈbɪljən ˈpipəl drɪŋk. moʊst əv ˈjuˈɛs kænt ˌɪˈmæʤən ə ˈbɑrbɪˌkju wɪˈθaʊt beer,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: a wedding without wine or a fancy soirée without champagne. We have
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: a* ˈwɛdɪŋ wɪˈθaʊt waɪn ər ə ˈfænsi soirée* wɪˈθaʊt ʃæmˈpeɪn. wi have

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: a drink at the weekend because it's the weekend. But also after work. When we
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: a* drɪŋk æt ðə ˈwiˌkɪnd bɪˈkəz ɪts ðə ˈwiˌkɪnd. bət ˈɔlsoʊ ˈæftər wərk. wɪn we

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: are stressed and when we are relaxed. Sad and happy. At home and outside.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: are* strɛst ənd wɪn wi ər rɪˈlækst. sæd ənd ˈhæpi. æt hoʊm ənd outside.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Alcohol is kind of a paradox. Why do we cling so much to the thing that harms us the most?
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: alcohol* ɪz kaɪnd əv ə ˈpɛrəˌdɑks. waɪ du wi klɪŋ soʊ məʧ tɪ ðə θɪŋ ðət hɑrmz ˈjuˈɛs ðə most?

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: You After One Drink
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: you* ˈæftər wən drink

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Alcohol is a biological weapon that yeast produces during fermentation to wipe out its competition.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: alcohol* ɪz ə ˌbaɪəˈlɑʤɪkəl ˈwɛpən ðət jist prəˈdusɪz ˈdʊrɪŋ ˌfərmənˈteɪʃən tɪ waɪp aʊt ɪts competition.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: In alcoholic drinks it’s mixed with water and flavored with everything from fruits to caramel.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: in* ˌælkəˈhɑlɪk drɪŋks it’s* mɪkst wɪθ ˈwɔtər ənd ˈfleɪvərd wɪθ ˈɛvriˌθɪŋ frəm fruts tɪ caramel.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: With just one sip, sextillions of alcohol molecules flood your stomach and small intestine.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: with* ʤɪst wən sɪp, sextillions* əv ˈælkəˌhɑl ˈmɑləˌkjulz fləd jʊr ˈstəmək ənd smɔl intestine.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: From there they head to your liver – your primary detox center, and to other organs like the brain.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: from* ðɛr ðeɪ hɛd tɪ jʊr ˈlɪvər –* jʊr ˈpraɪˌmɛri ˈdiˌtɑks ˈsɛnər, ənd tɪ ˈəðər ˈɔrgənz laɪk ðə breɪn.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: But your liver can only process about one sip of beer every 5 minutes. So unless you
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: bət jʊr ˈlɪvər kən ˈoʊnli ˈprɔˌsɛs əˈbaʊt wən sɪp əv bɪr ˈɛvəri 5 ˈmɪnəts. soʊ ənˈlɛs you

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: drink really slowly, it will become overwhelmed while an ever-growing swarm floods your brain.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: drink* ˈrɪli sˈloʊli, ɪt wɪl bɪˈkəm ˌoʊvərˈwɛlmd waɪl ən ever-growing* swɔrm flədz jʊr brain.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Here’s where the chaos begins. The legion of intruders fills your brain
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: here’s* wɛr ðə keɪɑs bɪˈgɪnz. ðə ˈliʤən əv ˌɪnˈtrudərz fɪlz jʊr brain

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: and starts messing with neurotransmitters and receptors. Their sabotage is so complex that
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: and* stɑrts ˈmɛsɪŋ wɪθ neurotransmitters* ənd rəˈsɛptərz. ðɛr ˈsæbəˌtɑʒ ɪz soʊ ˈkɑmplɛks that

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: scientists aren’t fully sure yet about all their mechanisms. But we know that
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: scientists* aren’t* ˈfʊli ʃʊr jɛt əˈbaʊt ɔl ðɛr ˈmɛkəˌnɪzəmz. bət wi noʊ that

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: alcohol numbs your neurons, making them slower and disrupts their communication.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: alcohol* numbs* jʊr ˈnʊrɑnz, ˈmeɪkɪŋ ðɛm sloʊər ənd dɪsˈrəpts ðɛr communication.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: This has several effects. It sedates you, melting away tension and stress. It stuns your prefrontal
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: this* həz ˈsɛvərəl ˈifɛkts. ɪt sɪˈdeɪts ju, ˈmɛltɪŋ əˈweɪ ˈtɛnʃən ənd strɛs. ɪt stənz jʊr prefrontal

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: cortex, your center for decision-making and self-control, making you more disinhibited
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: cortex*, jʊr ˈsɛnər fər dɪˈsɪʒənˌmeɪkɪŋ ənd ˌsɛlfkənˈtroʊl, ˈmeɪkɪŋ ju mɔr disinhibited

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: and prone to say or do things that you normally wouldn’t. And it releases endorphins – “feel good”
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: and* proʊn tɪ seɪ ər du θɪŋz ðət ju ˈnɔrməli wouldn’t*. ənd ɪt rɪˈlisɪz ɛnˈdɔrfɪnz –* ““feel* good”

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: molecules deeply tied to human connection. The chemicals that we produce when we laugh,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: molecules* ˈdipli taɪd tɪ ˈjumən kəˈnɛkʃən. ðə ˈkɛmɪkəlz ðət wi ˈproʊdus wɪn wi laugh,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: sing or dance with others, and which help us turn fleeting moments into shared memories.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: sing* ər dæns wɪθ ˈəðərz, ənd wɪʧ hɛlp ˈjuˈɛs tərn ˈflitɪŋ ˈmoʊmənts ˈɪntu ʃɛrd memories.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: So after a drink or three, the world doesn’t only feel lighter. It feels lighter with others. The
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: so* ˈæftər ə drɪŋk ər θri, ðə wərld doesn’t* ˈoʊnli fil ˈlaɪtər. ɪt filz ˈlaɪtər wɪθ ˈəðərz. the

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: invisible walls of insecurity around us begin to soften and you find yourself becoming a little
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: invisible* wɔlz əv ˌɪnsɪˈkjʊrɪti əraʊnd ˈjuˈɛs bɪˈgɪn tɪ ˈsɔfən ənd ju faɪnd ˈjɔrsɛlf bɪˈkəmɪŋ ə little

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: more… you. Less afraid to talk, laugh, sing or share joys and sorrows. Conversations flow easier,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: more…*… ju. lɛs əˈfreɪd tɪ tɔk, læf, sɪŋ ər ʃɛr ʤɔɪz ənd ˈsɑroʊz. ˌkɑnvərˈseɪʃənz floʊ easier,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: smiles last a little longer, and strangers can become friends or lovers more easily.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: smiles* læst ə ˈlɪtəl ˈlɔŋgər, ənd ˈstreɪnʤərz kən bɪˈkəm frɛndz ər ˈləvərz mɔr ˈizəli.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: So for a while, it feels like the weight of being human isn’t yours to carry alone.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: soʊ fər ə waɪl, ɪt filz laɪk ðə weɪt əv biɪŋ ˈjumən isn’t* jʊrz tɪ ˈkɛri alone.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Alcohol sits at a sweet spot of gentle relaxation, mild courage and friendly
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: alcohol* sɪts æt ə swit spɑt əv ˈʤɛnəl ˌrilækˈseɪʃən, maɪld kərɪʤ ənd friendly

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: companionship that makes it the perfect social lubricant. One that comes with quite a few costs.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: companionship* ðət meɪks ɪt ðə ˈpərˌfɪkt ˈsoʊʃəl ˈlubrəkənt. wən ðət kəmz wɪθ kwaɪt ə fju costs.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Your Body on Regular Drinking
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: your* ˈbɑdi ɔn ˈrɛgjələr drinking

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: For billions, these rounds become more frequent over time. The occasional drink becomes a weekly
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: for* ˈbɪljənz, ðiz raʊnz bɪˈkəm mɔr ˈfrikˌwɛnt ˈoʊvər taɪm. ðə ɔˈkeɪʒənəl drɪŋk bɪˈkəmz ə weekly

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: habit, then a ritual every other day, catching up with friends or unwinding.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: habit*, ðɛn ə ˈrɪʧuəl ˈɛvəri ˈəðər deɪ, ˈkæʧɪŋ əp wɪθ frɛndz ər unwinding.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: And there's always this buddy who drinks way more and has a perfectly normal life.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: and* ðɛrz ˈɔlˌweɪz ðɪs ˈbədi hu drɪŋks weɪ mɔr ənd həz ə ˈpərfəktli ˈnɔrməl life.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: But as the rounds add up over years, the damage builds up. The alcohol molecule
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: but* ɛz ðə raʊnz æd əp ˈoʊvər jɪrz, ðə ˈdæmɪʤ bɪldz əp. ðə ˈælkəˌhɑl molecule

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: can dissolve in water and fat, which allows it to invade almost every cell and tissue.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: can* dɪˈzɑlv ɪn ˈwɔtər ənd fæt, wɪʧ əˈlaʊz ɪt tɪ ˌɪnˈveɪd ˈɔlˌmoʊst ˈɛvəri sɛl ənd tissue.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: And when your body breaks it down, it transforms into acetaldehyde – a chemical that is even more
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: and* wɪn jʊr ˈbɑdi breɪks ɪt daʊn, ɪt trænsˈfɔrmz ˈɪntu acetaldehyde* –* ə ˈkɛmɪkəl ðət ɪz ˈivɪn more

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: toxic than alcohol itself and that wreaks havoc on your tissues, cells and DNA.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: toxic* ðən ˈælkəˌhɑl ˌɪtˈsɛlf ənd ðət riks ˈhævək ɔn jʊr ˈtɪʃuz, sɛlz ənd dna.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: In your brain, this shrinks your neurons and severs their connections,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: in* jʊr breɪn, ðɪs ʃrɪŋks jʊr ˈnʊrɑnz ənd ˈsɛvərz ðɛr connections,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: making it harder for different parts of the brain to communicate. As your brain withers,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: making* ɪt ˈhɑrdər fər ˈdɪfərənt pɑrts əv ðə breɪn tɪ kəmˈjunəˌkeɪt. ɛz jʊr breɪn withers,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: your memories fade, your thinking slows down and your risk of dementia increases.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: your* ˈmɛməriz feɪd, jʊr ˈθɪŋkɪŋ sloʊz daʊn ənd jʊr rɪsk əv dɪˈmɛnʃiə increases.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: When and how you drink matters – the younger you are, the wider the damage;
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: when* ənd haʊ ju drɪŋk ˈmætərz –* ðə ˈjəŋgər ju ər, ðə ˈwaɪdər ðə ˈdæmɪʤ;

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: and the more you drink in one go, the worse you’ll make it.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: ənd ðə mɔr ju drɪŋk ɪn wən goʊ, ðə wərs you’ll* meɪk it.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: The human brain isn’t fully wired until your mid 20s,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: the* ˈjumən breɪn isn’t* ˈfʊli waɪərd ənˈtɪl jʊr mɪd 20s,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: so drinking before that age is like smashing wet cement before it has set. In 20-year-olds,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: so* ˈdrɪŋkɪŋ ˌbiˈfɔr ðət eɪʤ ɪz laɪk sˈmæʃɪŋ wɛt sɪˈmɛnt ˌbiˈfɔr ɪt həz sɛt. ɪn 20-year-olds,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: blackout drinking episodes have been found to cause mental problems for up
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: blackout* ˈdrɪŋkɪŋ ˈɛpɪˌsoʊdz hæv bɪn faʊnd tɪ kɔz ˈmɛntəl ˈprɑbləmz fər up

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: to a year – forgetting why you entered a room or having difficulty learning new things. Binge
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: to* ə jɪr –* fərˈgɛtɪŋ waɪ ju ˈɛnərd ə rum ər ˈhævɪŋ ˈdɪfɪˌkəlti ˈlərnɪŋ nu θɪŋz. binge

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: drinking in late adolescence is also one of the highest risk factors for early-onset dementia.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: drinking* ɪn leɪt ˌædəˈlɛsəns ɪz ˈɔlsoʊ wən əv ðə haɪəst rɪsk ˈfæktərz fər early-onset* dementia.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Then there is cancer. Just as smoking hits your lungs, alcohol causes 8 types of
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: then* ðɛr ɪz ˈkænsər. ʤɪst ɛz sˈmoʊkɪŋ hɪts jʊr ləŋz, ˈælkəˌhɑl ˈkɔzɪz 8 taɪps of

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: cancer – basically everywhere from mouth to bowel, plus breast cancer in women. Here risks start at
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: cancer* –* ˈbeɪsɪkli ˈɛvriˌwɛr frəm maʊθ tɪ baʊəl, pləs brɛst ˈkænsər ɪn ˈwɪmən. hir rɪsks stɑrt at

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: an average consumption of less than 1 glass of wine per day. Beyond that, risks increase.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: an* ˈævərɪʤ kənˈsəmʃən əv lɛs ðən 1 glæs əv waɪn pər deɪ. bɪɔnd ðət, rɪsks increase.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Worldwide, alcohol causes around 740,000 new cancer cases per year, leading to 400,000 deaths.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: worldwide*, ˈælkəˌhɑl ˈkɔzɪz əraʊnd 740,000* nu ˈkænsər ˈkeɪsɪz pər jɪr, ˈlidɪŋ tɪ 400,000* deaths.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Another victim is your liver. Alcohol disrupts fat metabolism, causing fat to build up in your liver
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: another* ˈvɪktɪm ɪz jʊr ˈlɪvər. ˈælkəˌhɑl dɪsˈrəpts fæt məˈtæbəˌlɪzəm, ˈkɔzɪŋ fæt tɪ bɪld əp ɪn jʊr liver

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: cells. But this often has no symptoms, so you may go on drinking as your liver slowly turns to fat,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: cells*. bət ðɪs ˈɔfən həz noʊ ˈsɪmptəmz, soʊ ju meɪ goʊ ɔn ˈdrɪŋkɪŋ ɛz jʊr ˈlɪvər sˈloʊli tərnz tɪ fat,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: swells with inflammation and starts to fail. The final stage is cirrhosis – your liver is full of
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: swells* wɪθ ˌɪnfləˈmeɪʃən ənd stɑrts tɪ feɪl. ðə ˈfaɪnəl steɪʤ ɪz sərˈoʊsəs –* jʊr ˈlɪvər ɪz fʊl of

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: scars and barely functioning. This might take over 10 years, but once it appears it's largely
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: scars* ənd ˈbɛrli ˈfəŋkʃənɪŋ. ðɪs maɪt teɪk ˈoʊvər 10 jɪrz, bət wəns ɪt əˈpɪrz ɪts largely

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: irreversible. Every year, 600,000 people die because they have a liver destroyed by alcohol.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: irreversible*. ˈɛvəri jɪr, 600,000* ˈpipəl daɪ bɪˈkəz ðeɪ hæv ə ˈlɪvər dɪˈstrɔɪd baɪ ˈælkəˌhɑl.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Drinking also weakens your heart, raises blood pressure and increases the risk of
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: ˈdrɪŋkɪŋ ˈɔlsoʊ ˈwikənz jʊr hɑrt, ˈreɪzɪz bləd ˈprɛʃər ənd ˌɪnˈkrisɪz ðə rɪsk of

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: stroke and thrombosis – leading to another 500,000 deaths from cardiovascular diseases every year.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: stroke* ənd θrɑmˈboʊsəs –* ˈlidɪŋ tɪ əˈnəðər 500,000* dɛθs frəm ˌkɑrdioʊˈvæskjələr dɪˈzizɪz ˈɛvəri year.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: In terms of looks too, alcohol damages cells around your body, including your
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: in* tərmz əv lʊks tu, ˈælkəˌhɑl ˈdæmɪʤɪz sɛlz əraʊnd jʊr ˈbɑdi, ˌɪnˈkludɪŋ your

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: skin which looks older, sooner. And not only does alcohol contain a lot of calories itself,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: skin* wɪʧ lʊks ˈoʊldər, ˈsunər. ənd nɑt ˈoʊnli dɪz ˈælkəˌhɑl kənˈteɪn ə lɔt əv ˈkælɔˌriz itself,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: it also makes many people very hungry. Drinking is a huge source of weight gain and increases
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: it* ˈɔlsoʊ meɪks ˈmɛni ˈpipəl ˈvɛri ˈhəŋgri. ˈdrɪŋkɪŋ ɪz ə juʤ sɔrs əv weɪt geɪn ənd increases

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: the risk of obesity, opening the door to a cascade of other health problems.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: the* rɪsk əv əˈbisəti, ˈoʊpənɪŋ ðə dɔr tɪ ə kæˈskeɪd əv ˈəðər hɛlθ problems.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: When scientists crunch these numbers, they see that health problems can start
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: when* ˈsaɪəntɪsts krənʧ ðiz ˈnəmbərz, ðeɪ si ðət hɛlθ ˈprɑbləmz kən start

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: at about 1 beer a day, and that the chances of premature death start
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: at* əˈbaʊt 1 bɪr ə deɪ, ənd ðət ðə ˈʧænsɪz əv ˌpriməˈʧʊr dɛθ start

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: rising significantly at around 3 beers a day for men, and less than 2 for women.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: rising* sɪgˈnɪfɪkəntli æt əraʊnd 3 bɪrz ə deɪ fər mɛn, ənd lɛs ðən 2 fər women.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: If this sounds like a lot to you, maybe look around. In the EU,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: if* ðɪs saʊnz laɪk ə lɔt tɪ ju, ˈmeɪbi lʊk əraʊnd. ɪn ðə eu,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: It Isn’t Only Your Body
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: it* isn’t* ˈoʊnli jʊr body

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Alcohol is unique for another reason: its unmatched ability to destroy others.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Of those 400 million, more than half have fallen into an even deeper trap – dependence.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: In Europe and the Americas, 1 in 20 adults are caught in this web.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: The stereotype of alcoholism is one of extremes. A homeless person clutching

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: successful careers and families. As one of them you’ll show no antisocial
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: successful* kərɪrz ənd ˈfæməliz. ɛz wən əv ðɛm you’ll* ʃoʊ noʊ ˌæntɪˈsoʊʃəl

aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: bɪˈheɪvjər. you’ll* fʊlˈfɪl jʊr ˌɑbləˈgeɪʃənz. you’ll* ˈprɑbəˌbli hæv noʊ ˌdaɪəgˈnoʊst health

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: conditions. But on average you’ll be having 4 beers a day and will get drunk every week.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: conditions*. bət ɔn ˈævərɪʤ you’ll* bi ˈhævɪŋ 4 bɪrz ə deɪ ənd wɪl gɪt drəŋk ˈɛvəri week.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: And neither you nor your family may notice. No one questions a drink at dinner, after work,
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: and* ˈniðər ju nɔr jʊr ˈfæməli meɪ ˈnoʊtɪs. noʊ wən kˈwɛsʧənz ə drɪŋk æt ˈdɪnər, ˈæftər work,

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: We made a pact with alcohol to help us solve one of the greatest challenges of being human.

aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: human* ˌɪnərˈækʃən ɪz ˈmɛsi. wi lɔŋ fər ˈkloʊsnɪs. bət wi ər ˈɔlsoʊ ˈæŋʃəs, ˈɔkwərd, ənd ˈwɛri əv each

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: There are many ways to navigate this, alcohol is not “the” solution, but it is definitely one of
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: there* ər ˈmɛni weɪz tɪ ˈnævəˌgeɪt ðɪs, ˈælkəˌhɑl ɪz nɑt ““the”*” səˈluʃən, bət ɪt ɪz ˈdɛfənətli wən of

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Studies confirm what we have known for millennia. Modest quantities of alcohol
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: studies* kənˈfərm wət wi hæv noʊn fər məˈlɛniə. ˈmɑdəst kˈwɑntətiz əv alcohol

aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: help* ˈstreɪnʤərz bɑnd. ənd ˌɪndəˈvɪʤəwəlz hu drɪŋk ˈmɑdərətli ənd ˈsoʊʃəli tɛnd to

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: So if we want to step away from alcohol, we need to be honest about what we gain from it.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: so* ɪf wi wɔnt tɪ stɛp əˈweɪ frəm ˈælkəˌhɑl, wi nid tɪ bi ˈɑnəst əˈbaʊt wət wi geɪn frəm it.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: For millennia we’ve been giving it our health and years of our lives. And in return, alcohol gave us

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: confidence, connection, and celebration. But right now this is changing rapidly.

aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: binge* ˈdrɪŋkɪŋ ɪn ˌædəˈlɛsənts həz ˈpləmətɪd baɪ 50 ənd ˈivɪn ɪn ˈkəntriz laɪk ˈʤərməni ər france,

aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: where* bɪr ənd waɪn hæv ˈɔlˌweɪz bɪn pɑrt əv ðə ˈkəlʧərəl ˈdiˌɛˈneɪ, ˈælkəˌhɑl kənˈsəmʃən ɪz ˈfɑlɪŋ. and

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: that’s a victory. Fewer accidents. Fewer hospital beds filled. Fewer lives quietly derailed.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: 50%. Attendance at parties has fallen by 30%. Dating and casual sex have fallen
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: 50* əˈtɛndəns æt ˈpɑrtiz həz ˈfɑlən baɪ 30 ˈdeɪtɪŋ ənd ˈkæʒəwəl sɛks hæv fallen

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: There are many different reasons for this, from covid to social media. But the shift in which

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: drugs we consume is probably not totally unrelated either. Drugs like weed have gained ground
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: drugs* wi kənˈsum ɪz ˈprɑbəˌbli nɑt ˈtoʊtəli ˌənrɪˈleɪtɪd ˈiðər. drəgz laɪk wid hæv geɪnd ground

aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: massively* ənd ɪn ˈkɑntræst tɪ ˈdrɪŋkɪŋ, sˈmoʊkɪŋ wid tɛndz tɪ meɪk ˈmɛni ˈpipəl lɛs energetic

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: and likely to do things, more socially awkward and especially if you use a lot of it, more lonely.
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: and* ˈlaɪkli tɪ du θɪŋz, mɔr ˈsoʊʃəli ˈɔkwərd ənd əˈspɛʃəli ɪf ju juz ə lɔt əv ɪt, mɔr lonely.

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: Alcohol has been a powerful and influential tool for sharing, celebrating and enjoying life with
aOwmt39L2IQ.ipa: alcohol* həz bɪn ə ˈpaʊərfəl ənd ˌɪnfluˈɛnʃəl tul fər ˈʃɛrɪŋ, ˈsɛləˌbreɪtɪŋ ənd ˌɛnˈʤɔɪɪŋ laɪf wɪθ

aOwmt39L2IQ.text: others for thousands of years. If the West is on the path to drinking less, we might also be

ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /fɹˈʌm hˈiɹ tˈu ɡˈoʊ ənd lˈaɪv plˈeɪsəz ðˈæt hˈæv mˈɔɹ ˈækʃən mˈɔɹ dʒˈɑbz mˈɔɹ ˌɑpɚtˈunətiz lˈaɪk lˈʌndən fˈɔɹ ɪɡzˈæmpəl/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: But, it is really beautiful.
There probably is nowhere more beautiful.
>> [laughter] >> Are you in a rush, Wilson?
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /lˈæftɚ ˈɑɹ jˈu ɪn ə ɹˈʌʃ wˈɪlsən/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So pretty.
So, this part of the UK
has more of like a seasonal economy.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /hˈæz mˈɔɹ ˈʌv lˈaɪk ə sˈizənəl ɪkˈɑnəmi/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, a seasonal economy is where in the summer, it's really busy because we have lots of tourists, but in the winter, it's very quiet here.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ ə sˈizənəl ɪkˈɑnəmi ˈɪz wˈɛɹ ɪn ðə sˈʌmɚ ˈɪts ɹˈɪli bˈɪzi bɪkˈɔz wˈi hˈæv lˈɑts ˈʌv tˈʊɹəsts bˈʌt ɪn ðə wˈɪntɚ ˈɪts vˈɛɹi kwˈaɪət hˈiɹ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, people prefer to come in the summer, and they like to go to the beach,
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ pˈipəl pɹəfˈɝ tˈu kˈʌm ɪn ðə sˈʌmɚ ənd ðˈeɪ lˈaɪk tˈu ɡˈoʊ tˈu ðə bˈitʃ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: and things like this.
So, most people who live in Cornwall work in the tourism industry.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ mˈoʊst pˈipəl hˈu lˈaɪv ɪn kˈɔɹnwɔl wˈɝk ɪn ðə tˈʊɹˌɪzəm ˈɪndəstɹi/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, during the summer, they work every day, and then in the winter, it's kind of tricky to to find a job or find consistent employment unless
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ dˈʊɹɪŋ ðə sˈʌmɚ ðˈeɪ wˈɝk ˈɛvɚi dˈeɪ ənd ðˈɛn ɪn ðə wˈɪntɚ ˈɪts kˈaɪnd ˈʌv tɹˈɪki tˈu tˈu fˈaɪnd ə dʒˈɑb ˈɔɹ fˈaɪnd kənsˈɪstənt ɛmplˈɔɪmənt ənlˈɛs/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: you work in a school, or a supermarket,
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /jˈu wˈɝk ɪn ə skˈul ˈɔɹ ə sˈupɚmˌɑɹkɪt/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: or maybe you are an accountant, but we do not have many like
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈɔɹ mˈeɪbi jˈu ˈɑɹ ˈæn əkˈaʊntənt bˈʌt wˈi dˈu nˈɑt hˈæv mˈɛni lˈaɪk/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: businessmen here unless you own your tourism business.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /bˈɪznɪsmˌɛn hˈiɹ ənlˈɛs jˈu ˈoʊn jˈɔɹ tˈʊɹˌɪzəm bˈɪznəs/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Another thing is in the UK, we love to drink.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənˈʌðɚ θˈɪŋ ˈɪz ɪn ðə jˌukˈeɪ wˈi lˈʌv tˈu dɹˈɪŋk/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: We love beer.
We love wine.
Here in Cornwall, we are famous for our cider.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /hˈiɹ ɪn kˈɔɹnwɔl wˈi ˈɑɹ fˈeɪməs fˈɔɹ ˈaʊɚ sˈaɪdɚ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, nearly every village has a pub, um like this one here in this little town I'm in.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ nˈɪɹli ˈɛvɚi vˈɪlədʒ hˈæz ə pˈʌb ˈʌm lˈaɪk ðˈɪs wˈʌn hˈiɹ ɪn ðˈɪs lˈɪtəl tˈaʊn ˈaɪm ɪn/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: And in the summer, especially when we have a sunny day, the pub is really busy.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənd ɪn ðə sˈʌmɚ əspˈɛʃli wˈɛn wˈi hˈæv ə sˈʌni dˈeɪ ðə pˈʌb ˈɪz ɹˈɪli bˈɪzi/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, it's not like in London, where people go for a drink after work.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ ˈɪts nˈɑt lˈaɪk ɪn lˈʌndən wˈɛɹ pˈipəl ɡˈoʊ fˈɔɹ ə dɹˈɪŋk ˈæftɚ wˈɝk/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Here, it's when we have nice weather, or if you want to sit inside by the fire, it's nice to go to a pub as well.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /hˈiɹ ˈɪts wˈɛn wˈi hˈæv nˈaɪs wˈɛðɚ ˈɔɹ ˈɪf jˈu wˈɑnt tˈu sˈɪt ˌɪnsˈaɪd bˈaɪ ðə fˈaɪɚ ˈɪts nˈaɪs tˈu ɡˈoʊ tˈu ə pˈʌb ˈæz wˈɛl/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: It's really nice weather today.
Super tranquil.
There's even this super cute
old tractor.
We recently had a storm in the UK,
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /wˈi ɹˈisəntli hˈæd ə stˈɔɹm ɪn ðə jˌukˈeɪ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: and lots of trees fell down, including
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənd lˈɑts ˈʌv tɹˈiz fˈɛl dˈaʊn ˌɪnklˈudɪŋ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: here, right in my path.
So, I think we have to go
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ ˈaɪ θˈɪŋk wˈi hˈæv tˈu ɡˈoʊ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: underneath.
Underneath the fallen tree, but there are so many trees down, and I've been seeing them as I've walked around today.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˌʌndɚnˈiθ ðə fˈɑlən tɹˈi bˈʌt ðˈɛɹ ˈɑɹ sˈoʊ mˈɛni tɹˈiz dˈaʊn ənd ˈaɪv bˈɪn sˈiɪŋ ðˈɛm ˈæz ˈaɪv wˈɔkt ɚˈaʊnd tədˈeɪ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, we're now going to go for a walk with with Wilson to try and get rid of some extra energy.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ wˈiɹ nˈaʊ ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu ɡˈoʊ fˈɔɹ ə wˈɔk wˈɪð wˈɪð wˈɪlsən tˈu tɹˈaɪ ənd ɡˈɛt ɹˈɪd ˈʌv sˈʌm ˈɛkstɹə ˈɛnɚdʒi/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, let's go through the forest.
One more tree here.
One tree there.
And here, it's so crazy.
And another one up there.
I'll see if I can show you.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈaɪl sˈi ˈɪf ˈaɪ kˈæn ʃˈoʊ jˈu/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Can you see this tree
completely Ooh.
Snapped.
It snapped.
So, the storm was The
storm was really really strong.
Really powerful.
So, hopefully There's another one.
So many fallen trees.
It really smells here like wild garlic.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈɪt ɹˈɪli smˈɛlz hˈiɹ lˈaɪk wˈaɪld ɡˈɑɹlɪk/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: You can see it growing in the hedgerows.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /jˈu kˈæn sˈi ˈɪt ɡɹˈoʊɪŋ ɪn ðə hedgerows/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: It's here.
And it smells really strong of garlic.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənd ˈɪt smˈɛlz ɹˈɪli stɹˈɔŋ ˈʌv ɡˈɑɹlɪk/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Kind of gross.
Somebody has parked their boat here, and I think you can see it's just started to rain.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈʌmbˌɑdi hˈæz pˈɑɹkt ðˈɛɹ bˈoʊt hˈiɹ ənd ˈaɪ θˈɪŋk jˈu kˈæn sˈi ˈɪts dʒˈʌst stˈɑɹtɪd tˈu ɹˈeɪn/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: >> [laughter] >> He hates the rain, so
I think we're going to walk down to the sea, and then turn back.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈaɪ θˈɪŋk wˈiɹ ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu wˈɔk dˈaʊn tˈu ðə sˈi ənd ðˈɛn tˈɝn bˈæk/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Another very common thing to see in the countryside is people selling eggs.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənˈʌðɚ vˈɛɹi kˈɑmən θˈɪŋ tˈu sˈi ɪn ðə kˈʌntɹisˌaɪd ˈɪz pˈipəl sˈɛlɪŋ ˈɛɡz/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, they're selling eggs, and they have a little honesty pot where you put your money.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ ðˈɛɹ sˈɛlɪŋ ˈɛɡz ənd ðˈeɪ hˈæv ə lˈɪtəl ˈɑnəsti pˈɑt wˈɛɹ jˈu pˈʊt jˈɔɹ mˈʌni/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Okay.
So, we just got back in the car.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ wˈi dʒˈʌst ɡˈɑt bˈæk ɪn ðə kˈɑɹ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um unfortunately, unfortunately, it started raining.
So, Wilson hates the rain.
I hate the rain.
I think we enjoyed the sunshine while it lasted, while we had it.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈaɪ θˈɪŋk wˈi ˌɛndʒˈɔɪd ðə sˈʌnʃˌaɪn wˈaɪl ˈɪt lˈæstəd wˈaɪl wˈi hˈæd ˈɪt/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um but now it's time to to go back back home, back inside, dry off.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm bˈʌt nˈaʊ ˈɪts tˈaɪm tˈu tˈu ɡˈoʊ bˈæk bˈæk hˈoʊm bˈæk ˌɪnsˈaɪd dɹˈaɪ ˈɔf/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um I have a few other things to do today, but my main goal was enjoy the sunshine.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm ˈaɪ hˈæv ə fjˈu ˈʌðɚ θˈɪŋz tˈu dˈu tədˈeɪ bˈʌt mˈaɪ mˈeɪn ɡˈoʊl wˈɑz ˌɛndʒˈɔɪ ðə sˈʌnʃˌaɪn/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: And I think I did that for as long as I could.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənd ˈaɪ θˈɪŋk ˈaɪ dˈɪd ðˈæt fˈɔɹ ˈæz lˈɔŋ ˈæz ˈaɪ kˈʊd/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um but unfortunately,
we have still had rain every day of 2026
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /wˈi hˈæv stˈɪl hˈæd ɹˈeɪn ˈɛvɚi dˈeɪ ˈʌv 2026/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: in the UK.
So, what can you do?
What can you do?
I don't know.
So, me and Wilson are going to go home.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ mˈi ənd wˈɪlsən ˈɑɹ ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu ɡˈoʊ hˈoʊm/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: He's drying himself right now.
I don't know if you can see.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈaɪ dˈoʊnt nˈoʊ ˈɪf jˈu kˈæn sˈi/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, we are going to head back.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ wˈi ˈɑɹ ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu hˈɛd bˈæk/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: >> [music] >> I'm here at the supermarket to get some things for
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /mjˈuzɪk ˈaɪm hˈiɹ ˈæt ðə sˈupɚmˌɑɹkɪt tˈu ɡˈɛt sˈʌm θˈɪŋz fˈɔɹ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: dinner, um to get something to eat.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /dˈɪnɚ ˈʌm tˈu ɡˈɛt sˈʌmθɪŋ tˈu ˈit/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: And basically, here in the UK, we have four main supermarkets called the big four.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənd bˈeɪsɪkli hˈiɹ ɪn ðə jˌukˈeɪ wˈi hˈæv fˈɔɹ mˈeɪn sˈupɚmˌɑɹkɪts kˈɔld ðə bˈɪɡ fˈɔɹ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, I'm here at Tesco, and this is one of the kind of big four supermarkets.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ ˈaɪm hˈiɹ ˈæt tˈɛskoʊ ənd ðˈɪs ˈɪz wˈʌn ˈʌv ðə kˈaɪnd ˈʌv bˈɪɡ fˈɔɹ sˈupɚmˌɑɹkɪts/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: But, also, there are two more, Lidl's and Aldi, which are very very popular, um as well.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /bˈʌt ˈɔlsoʊ ðˈɛɹ ˈɑɹ tˈu mˈɔɹ lidl's ənd ˈɑldi wˈɪtʃ ˈɑɹ vˈɛɹi vˈɛɹi pˈɑpjəlɚ ˈʌm ˈæz wˈɛl/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, I'm going to go inside, get some food for for dinner, and um I'll see you in a moment.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ ˈaɪm ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu ɡˈoʊ ˌɪnsˈaɪd ɡˈɛt sˈʌm fˈud fˈɔɹ fˈɔɹ dˈɪnɚ ənd ˈʌm ˈaɪl sˈi jˈu ɪn ə mˈoʊmənt/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Okay, we just got home, and when Wilson goes out, he thinks he needs a reward.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˌoʊkˈeɪ wˈi dʒˈʌst ɡˈɑt hˈoʊm ənd wˈɛn wˈɪlsən ɡˈoʊz ˈaʊt hˈi θˈɪŋks hˈi nˈidz ə ɹɪwˈɔɹd/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Do you want a treat?
Do you want a treat?
He's going to snatch it.
Gentle.
Gentle.
So, now um that we're back home, I think I'm going to chill for a bit.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ nˈaʊ ˈʌm ðˈæt wˈiɹ bˈæk hˈoʊm ˈaɪ θˈɪŋk ˈaɪm ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu tʃˈɪl fˈɔɹ ə bˈɪt/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: I'm just going to just going to relax.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈaɪm dʒˈʌst ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu dʒˈʌst ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu ɹɪlˈæks/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, I'll see you soon.
So, I've got some things for for dinner.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ ˈaɪv ɡˈɑt sˈʌm θˈɪŋz fˈɔɹ fˈɔɹ dˈɪnɚ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, I'm having pie.
We love pies.
The UK, we love pies.
In particular, this brand, Pukka, we love Pukka pies.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ɪn pɚtˈɪkjəlɚ ðˈɪs bɹˈænd pukka wˈi lˈʌv pukka pˈaɪz/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um I'm vegetarian, so mine is vegan.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm ˈaɪm vˌɛdʒətˈɛɹˌiən sˈoʊ mˈaɪn ˈɪz vˈɛɡən/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: And I will make that with mashed potatoes,
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənd ˈaɪ wˈɪl mˈeɪk ðˈæt wˈɪð mˈæʃt pətˈeɪtoʊz/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: and cabbage and leek.
Here.
But, the the other thing I wanted to show you is in British supermarkets right now, we have lots of things for Easter.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /bˈʌt ðə ðə ˈʌðɚ θˈɪŋ ˈaɪ wˈɔntɪd tˈu ʃˈoʊ jˈu ˈɪz ɪn bɹˈɪtɪʃ sˈupɚmˌɑɹkɪts ɹˈaɪt nˈaʊ wˈi hˈæv lˈɑts ˈʌv θˈɪŋz fˈɔɹ ˈistɚ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: And in the UK, we normally celebrate Easter with eggs because an egg simila- symbolizes
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənd ɪn ðə jˌukˈeɪ wˈi nˈɔɹməli sˈɛləbɹˌeɪt ˈistɚ wˈɪð ˈɛɡz bɪkˈɔz ˈæn ˈɛɡ simila sˈɪmbəlˌaɪzɪz/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: an egg symbolizes a new beginning.
So, one of the brands of chocolate eggs that we have
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ wˈʌn ˈʌv ðə bɹˈændz ˈʌv tʃˈɔklət ˈɛɡz ðˈæt wˈi hˈæv/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: is this one by Cadbury, which is I think it's a British chocolate brand.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈɪz ðˈɪs wˈʌn bˈaɪ kˈædbˌɛɹi wˈɪtʃ ˈɪz ˈaɪ θˈɪŋk ˈɪts ə bɹˈɪtɪʃ tʃˈɔklət bɹˈænd/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um and they make these things called cream eggs, which are basically like chocolate eggs with icing inside.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm ənd ðˈeɪ mˈeɪk ðˈiz θˈɪŋz kˈɔld kɹˈim ˈɛɡz wˈɪtʃ ˈɑɹ bˈeɪsɪkli lˈaɪk tʃˈɔklət ˈɛɡz wˈɪð ˈaɪsɪŋ ˌɪnsˈaɪd/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: They have icing inside, so really sugary.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ðˈeɪ hˈæv ˈaɪsɪŋ ˌɪnsˈaɪd sˈoʊ ɹˈɪli ʃˈʊɡɚi/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um and it looks like an egg.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm ənd ˈɪt lˈʊks lˈaɪk ˈæn ˈɛɡ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Can you see?
So, these are like a classic.
Everyone in the UK knows what a cream egg is.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈɛvɹiwˌʌn ɪn ðə jˌukˈeɪ nˈoʊz wˈʌt ə kɹˈim ˈɛɡ ˈɪz/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um the normal one, the kind of classic one, is with milk chocolate.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm ðə nˈɔɹməl wˈʌn ðə kˈaɪnd ˈʌv klˈæsɪk wˈʌn ˈɪz wˈɪð mˈɪlk tʃˈɔklət/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: But, they also do them with white chocolate, which I think is new.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /bˈʌt ðˈeɪ ˈɔlsoʊ dˈu ðˈɛm wˈɪð wˈaɪt tʃˈɔklət wˈɪtʃ ˈaɪ θˈɪŋk ˈɪz nˈu/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: This is new with white chocolate.
And they have a caramel egg, but that's a completely
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ənd ðˈeɪ hˈæv ə kˈɛɹəməl ˈɛɡ bˈʌt ðˈæts ə kəmplˈitli/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: different thing.
So, basically, my plans for the rest of the day are it's raining, so I'm going to cook dinner.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ bˈeɪsɪkli mˈaɪ plˈænz fˈɔɹ ðə ɹˈɛst ˈʌv ðə dˈeɪ ˈɑɹ ˈɪts ɹˈeɪnɪŋ sˈoʊ ˈaɪm ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu kˈʊk dˈɪnɚ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: I'm going to cook pie.
I'm going to light the fire and um I'm going to chill, but I wanted to show you.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈaɪm ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu lˈaɪt ðə fˈaɪɚ ənd ˈʌm ˈaɪm ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu tʃˈɪl bˈʌt ˈaɪ wˈɔntɪd tˈu ʃˈoʊ jˈu/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: So, the cream egg is chocolate.
My phone ran out of storage.
But, this is the cream egg.
I just wanted to show you, but it's really good.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈaɪ dʒˈʌst wˈɔntɪd tˈu ʃˈoʊ jˈu bˈʌt ˈɪts ɹˈɪli ɡˈʊd/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um Yeah.
But, honestly, I think this video is going to probably end here.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /bˈʌt ˈɑnəstli ˈaɪ θˈɪŋk ðˈɪs vˈɪdioʊ ˈɪz ɡˈoʊɪŋ tˈu pɹˈɑbəblˌi ˈɛnd hˈiɹ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um I wanted to say thank you so much for your support, for your comments, for liking videos, and subscribing.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm ˈaɪ wˈɔntɪd tˈu sˈeɪ θˈæŋk jˈu sˈoʊ mˈʌtʃ fˈɔɹ jˈɔɹ səpˈɔɹt fˈɔɹ jˈɔɹ kˈɑmɛnts fˈɔɹ lˈaɪkɪŋ vˈɪdioʊz ənd səbskɹˈaɪbɪŋ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um I'm completely new to YouTube, so this is my first time making a channel, and I have no idea
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm ˈaɪm kəmplˈitli nˈu tˈu jˈutjˌub sˈoʊ ðˈɪs ˈɪz mˈaɪ fˈɝst tˈaɪm mˈeɪkɪŋ ə tʃˈænəl ənd ˈaɪ hˈæv nˈoʊ aɪdˈiə/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: what I'm doing.
I have no idea.
So, I really, really appreciate your support.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /sˈoʊ ˈaɪ ɹˈɪli ɹˈɪli əpɹˈiʃiˌeɪt jˈɔɹ səpˈɔɹt/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um please do let me know in the comments what videos you would like to see.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm plˈiz dˈu lˈɛt mˈi nˈoʊ ɪn ðə kˈɑmɛnts wˈʌt vˈɪdioʊz jˈu wˈʊd lˈaɪk tˈu sˈi/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: What videos do you want to see?
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /wˈʌt vˈɪdioʊz dˈu jˈu wˈɑnt tˈu sˈi/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um what videos do you like watching?
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm wˈʌt vˈɪdioʊz dˈu jˈu lˈaɪk wˈɑtʃɪŋ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Um but other than that, thank you so, so much for watching this video.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈʌm bˈʌt ˈʌðɚ ðˈæn ðˈæt θˈæŋk jˈu sˈoʊ sˈoʊ mˈʌtʃ fˈɔɹ wˈɑtʃɪŋ ðˈɪs vˈɪdioʊ/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: I will see you in the next one.
ohkDb6xNSas.ipa: /ˈaɪ wˈɪl sˈi jˈu ɪn ðə nˈɛkst wˈʌn/

ohkDb6xNSas.text: Bye.
>> [music]

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You were on your way home, when you died.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈu wˈɝ ˈɑn jˈɔɹ wˈeɪ hˈoʊm wˈɛn jˈu dˈaɪd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: The medics tried their best to save you... but to no avail.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ðə mˈɛdɪks tɹˈaɪd ðˈɛɹ bˈɛst tˈu sˈeɪv jˈu bˈʌt tˈu nˈoʊ əvˈeɪl/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Your body was so utterly shattered, you were better off. Trust me.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ bˈɑdi wˈɑz sˈoʊ ˈʌtɚli ʃˈætɚd jˈu wˈɝ bˈɛtɚ ˈɔf tɹˈʌst mˈi/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: And that's when you met me.
There was... there was a truck.
But don't feel bad about it.
"That's what I like to see," I said.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ðˈæts wˈʌt ˈaɪ lˈaɪk tˈu sˈi ˈaɪ sˈɛd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You just died, and your main concern is for your family.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈu dʒˈʌst dˈaɪd ənd jˈɔɹ mˈeɪn kənsˈɝn ˈɪz fˈɔɹ jˈɔɹ fˈæməli/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You looked at me with fascination.
To you, I didn't look like God.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /tˈu jˈu ˈaɪ dˈɪdənt lˈʊk lˈaɪk ɡˈɑd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: I just looked like some man, or possibly a woman.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈaɪ dʒˈʌst lˈʊkt lˈaɪk sˈʌm mˈæn ˈɔɹ pˈɑsəbli ə wˈʊmən/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Your kids will remember you as perfect in every way.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ kˈɪdz wˈɪl ɹɪmˈɛmbɚ jˈu ˈæz pɚfˈɛkt ɪn ˈɛvɚi wˈeɪ/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: They didn't have time to grow contemptuous of you.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ðˈeɪ dˈɪdənt hˈæv tˈaɪm tˈu ɡɹˈoʊ kəntˈɛmptʃuəs ˈʌv jˈu/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Your wife will cry on the outside.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ wˈaɪf wˈɪl kɹˈaɪ ˈɑn ðə ˈaʊtsˈaɪd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: To be fair, your marriage was falling apart.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /tˈu bˈi fˈɛɹ jˈɔɹ mˈɛɹɪdʒ wˈɑz fˈɑlɪŋ əpˈɑɹt/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: If it's any consolation, she'll feel very guilty for feeling relieved.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈɪf ˈɪts ˈɛni kˌɑnsəlˈeɪʃən ʃˈil fˈil vˈɛɹi ɡˈɪlti fˈɔɹ fˈilɪŋ ɹɪlˈivd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Do I go to Heaven? Or Hell or something?
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /dˈu ˈaɪ ɡˈoʊ tˈu hˈɛvən ˈɔɹ hˈɛl ˈɔɹ sˈʌmθɪŋ/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: All religions are right in their own way.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈɔl ɹilˈɪdʒənz ˈɑɹ ɹˈaɪt ɪn ðˈɛɹ ˈoʊn wˈeɪ/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You followed along as we strolled through the void.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈu fˈɑloʊd əlˈɔŋ ˈæz wˈi stɹˈoʊld θɹˈu ðə vˈɔɪd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: It's just nice to walk while we talk.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈɪts dʒˈʌst nˈaɪs tˈu wˈɔk wˈaɪl wˈi tˈɔk/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: When I get reborn, I'll just be a blank slate right?
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /wˈɛn ˈaɪ ɡˈɛt ɹˈibˈɔɹn ˈaɪl dʒˈʌst bˈi ə blˈæŋk slˈeɪt ɹˈaɪt/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: So, all my experiences and everything... everything I did in this life...
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /sˈoʊ ˈɔl mˈaɪ ɪkspˈɪɹiənsɪz ənd ˈɛvɹiθˌɪŋ ˈɛvɹiθˌɪŋ ˈaɪ dˈɪd ɪn ðˈɪs lˈaɪf/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You have within you, all the knowledge and experiences of all your past lives.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈu hˈæv wɪðˈɪn jˈu ˈɔl ðə nˈɑlədʒ ənd ɪkspˈɪɹiənsɪz ˈʌv ˈɔl jˈɔɹ pˈæst lˈɪvz/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You just don't remember them right now.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈu dʒˈʌst dˈoʊnt ɹɪmˈɛmbɚ ðˈɛm ɹˈaɪt nˈaʊ/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: I stopped walking, and took you by the shoulders.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈaɪ stˈɑpt wˈɔkɪŋ ənd tˈʊk jˈu bˈaɪ ðə ʃˈoʊldɚz/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Your soul is more magnificent, beautiful, and gigantic than you can possibly imagine.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈɔɹ sˈoʊl ˈɪz mˈɔɹ mæɡnˈɪfəsənt bjˈutəfəl ənd dʒaɪɡˈæntɪk ðˈæn jˈu kˈæn pˈɑsəbli ˌɪmˈædʒən/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: A human mind can only contain a tiny fraction of what you are.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ə hjˈumən mˈaɪnd kˈæn ˈoʊnli kəntˈeɪn ə tˈaɪni fɹˈækʃən ˈʌv wˈʌt jˈu ˈɑɹ/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: It's like sticking your finger in a glass of water.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈɪts lˈaɪk stˈɪkɪŋ jˈɔɹ fˈɪŋɡɚ ɪn ə ɡlˈæs ˈʌv wˈɔtɚ/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: To see if it's hot or cold.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /tˈu sˈi ˈɪf ˈɪts hˈɑt ˈɔɹ kˈoʊld/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You put a tiny part of yourself into the vessel, and when you bring it back out, you've gained all the experiences it had.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈu pˈʊt ə tˈaɪni pˈɑɹt ˈʌv jɚsˈɛlf ˈɪntu ðə vˈɛsəl ənd wˈɛn jˈu bɹˈɪŋ ˈɪt bˈæk ˈaʊt jˈuv ɡˈeɪnd ˈɔl ðə ɪkspˈɪɹiənsɪz ˈɪt hˈæd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You've been in a human for the last 48 years.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈuv bˈɪn ɪn ə hjˈumən fˈɔɹ ðə lˈæst 48 jˈɪɹz/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: So you haven't stretched out yet and felt the rest of your immense consciousness.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /sˈoʊ jˈu hˈævənt stɹˈɛtʃt ˈaʊt jˈɛt ənd fˈɛlt ðə ɹˈɛst ˈʌv jˈɔɹ ˌɪmˈɛns kˈɑnʃəsnəs/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: If we hung out here for long enough, you'd start remembering everything.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈɪf wˈi hˈʌŋ ˈaʊt hˈiɹ fˈɔɹ lˈɔŋ ɪnˈʌf jˈud stˈɑɹt ɹɪmˈɛmbɚɪŋ ˈɛvɹiθˌɪŋ/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: But there's not point to doing that between each life.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /bˈʌt ðˈɛɹz nˈɑt pˈɔɪnt tˈu dˈuɪŋ ðˈæt bɪtwˈin ˈitʃ lˈaɪf/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: How many times have I been reincarnated then?
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /hˈaʊ mˈɛni tˈaɪmz hˈæv ˈaɪ bˈɪn ɹˌiɪnkˈɑɹnˌeɪtɪd ðˈɛn/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: And into lots of different lives.
This time around, you'll be a Chinese peasant girl in 540 A.D.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ðˈɪs tˈaɪm ɚˈaʊnd jˈul bˈi ə tʃaɪnˈiz pˈɛzənt ɡˈɝl ɪn 540 ˈæd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You're sending me back in time?
Time as you know it, only exists in your universe.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /tˈaɪm ˈæz jˈu nˈoʊ ˈɪt ˈoʊnli ɪɡzˈɪsts ɪn jˈɔɹ jˈunəvˌɝs/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Things are different where I come from.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /θˈɪŋz ˈɑɹ dˈɪfɚənt wˈɛɹ ˈaɪ kˈʌm fɹˈʌm/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Oh sure, I come from somewhere. Somewhere else.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈoʊ ʃˈʊɹ ˈaɪ kˈʌm fɹˈʌm sˈʌmwˌɛɹ sˈʌmwˌɛɹ ˈɛls/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: And there are others like me.
I know you'll want to know what it's like there but honestly you wouldn't understand.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈaɪ nˈoʊ jˈul wˈɑnt tˈu nˈoʊ wˈʌt ˈɪts lˈaɪk ðˈɛɹ bˈʌt ˈɑnəstli jˈu wˈʊdənt ˌʌndɚstˈænd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You said, a little let down.
If I get reincarnated to other places in time, I could have interacted with myself at some point.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈɪf ˈaɪ ɡˈɛt ɹˌiɪnkˈɑɹnˌeɪtɪd tˈu ˈʌðɚ plˈeɪsəz ɪn tˈaɪm ˈaɪ kˈʊd hˈæv ˌɪntɚˈæktəd wˈɪð mˌaɪsˈɛlf ˈæt sˈʌm pˈɔɪnt/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: And with both lives only aware of their own lifespans, you don't even know it's happening.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ənd wˈɪð bˈoʊθ lˈɪvz ˈoʊnli əwˈɛɹ ˈʌv ðˈɛɹ ˈoʊn lˈaɪfspˌænz jˈu dˈoʊnt ˈivɪn nˈoʊ ˈɪts hˈæpənɪŋ/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: what's the point of it all?
I looked you in the eye.
the reason I made this whole universe,
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ðə ɹˈizən ˈaɪ mˈeɪd ðˈɪs hˈoʊl jˈunəvˌɝs/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You want to us to mature?
I made this whole universe for you.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈaɪ mˈeɪd ðˈɪs hˈoʊl jˈunəvˌɝs fˈɔɹ jˈu/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: you grow and mature, and become a larger and greater intellect.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈu ɡɹˈoʊ ənd mətʃˈʊɹ ənd bɪkˈʌm ə lˈɑɹdʒɚ ənd ɡɹˈeɪtɚ ˈɪntəlˌɛkt/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: In this universe, there's just you and me.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ɪn ðˈɪs jˈunəvˌɝs ðˈɛɹz dʒˈʌst jˈu ənd mˈi/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: But all the people on Earth...
I'm every human being who ever lived?
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈaɪm ˈɛvɚi hjˈumən bˈiɪŋ hˈu ˈɛvɚ lˈɪvd/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Or who will ever live,  yes.
And you're John Wilkes Booth too.
And you're the millions he killed.
And you're everyone who followed Him.
Every time you victimized someone, you were victimizing yourself.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈɛvɚi tˈaɪm jˈu vˈɪktəmaɪzd sˈʌmwˌʌn jˈu wˈɝ vˈɪktəmaɪzɪŋ jɚsˈɛlf/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Every act of kindness you have done, you have done to yourself.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈɛvɚi ˈækt ˈʌv kˈaɪndnəs jˈu hˈæv dˈʌn jˈu hˈæv dˈʌn tˈu jɚsˈɛlf/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: Every happy and sad moment ever experienced by any human was or will be experienced by you.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ˈɛvɚi hˈæpi ənd sˈæd mˈoʊmənt ˈɛvɚ ɪkspˈɪɹiənst bˈaɪ ˈɛni hjˈumən wˈɑz ˈɔɹ wˈɪl bˈi ɪkspˈɪɹiənst bˈaɪ jˈu/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: You thought for a long time.
Because someday you will become like me.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /bɪkˈɔz sˈʌmdˌeɪ jˈu wˈɪl bɪkˈʌm lˈaɪk mˈi/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: No, not yet, you're a fetus.
Once you've lived every human life throughout all time,
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /wˈʌns jˈuv lˈɪvd ˈɛvɚi hjˈumən lˈaɪf θɹuˈaʊt ˈɔl tˈaɪm/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: you will have grown enough to be born.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /jˈu wˈɪl hˈæv ɡɹˈoʊn ɪnˈʌf tˈu bˈi bˈɔɹn/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: now it's time for you to move on to your next life.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /nˈaʊ ˈɪts tˈaɪm fˈɔɹ jˈu tˈu mˈuv ˈɑn tˈu jˈɔɹ nˈɛkst lˈaɪf/

h6fcK_fRYaI.text: And I sent you on your way.
h6fcK_fRYaI.ipa: /ənd ˈaɪ sˈɛnt jˈu ˈɑn jˈɔɹ wˈeɪ/

Hyjol8eLb3A.text: hello everyone my name is Maria and I'm Laurie and we are the hosts of the IELTS speaking for success podcast the podcast aims to help you improve
Hyjol8eLb3A.ipa: /həlˈoʊ ˈɛvɹiwˌʌn mˈaɪ nˈeɪm ˈɪz mɚˈiə ənd ˈaɪm lˈɔɹi ənd wˈi ˈɑɹ ðə hˈoʊsts ˈʌv ðə ielts spˈikɪŋ fˈɔɹ səksˈɛs pˈɔdkˌæst ðə pˈɔdkˌæst ˈeɪmz tˈu hˈɛlp jˈu ˌɪmpɹˈuv/
